owned by [[Mori Nagayoshi]] ([https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 Human Mukotsu replica forged by Akō swordsmith Sasaki Noriyuki during the late Edo period]). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|Ōishi Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Akō City buildings]] [[Category:History of Akō City]] [[Category:Akō incident]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Oishi Shrine]] [[Category:forcing refresh for Confirmed wikipedia 11s 6]] [[Category: of the shrine buildings in [[1935]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture Former Fusha]] [[Category:Akō Incident]] [[Category:Ōishi Yoshio]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Oishi Shrine]] [[ja:大石神社]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Oishi Shrine]]

{{Otheruses}} '''大石神社'''（おおいしじんじゃ）は、[[赤穂事件]]において討ち入りをした[[大石良雄]]ら[[赤穂事件の人物一覧|赤穂浪士]]（赤穂義士）を祀る[[神社]]。[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]にはばかって表立って顕彰することはできなかったが、[[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年）、[[明治天皇]]が赤穂浪士の墓のある[[泉岳寺]]に勅使を遣わし、これを弔って以降、赤穂城内に神社を作ろうとする動きが起きた。大正から昭和初期に[[赤穂市]]と[[京都市]]に赤穂浪士を祀る神社が創建された。江戸時代後期から流行していた[[藩祖]]を祀った神社のひとつ。 == 赤穂大石神社 == {{神社 | 名称=赤穂大石神社 | 画像=[[ファイル:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan02s3.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[兵庫県]][[赤穂市]]上仮屋旧城内 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E|}} | 祭神=大石内蔵助良雄以下四十七士命、[[萱野重実|萱野三平]]
浅野家三代
森家七将 | 社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[1900年]]（[[明治]]33年） | 札所等=[[神仏霊場巡拝の道]]第76番（兵庫第11番） | 例祭=[[12月14日]]（[[赤穂義士祭]]） | 地図={{Location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=57|lon_deg=134|lon_min=23|lon_sec=19|label=赤穂大石神社|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}}}} }} {{Commonscat|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[兵庫県]][[赤穂市]]の[[赤穂城]]の中にある。赤穂神社と大石神社が統合されたもの。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は県社で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。旧・赤穂神社祭神の[[赤穂藩]]主[[浅野氏|浅野家]]・[[森氏|森家]]祖霊と旧・大石神社祭神の[[大石良雄]]ら[[赤穂事件の人物一覧|赤穂浪士]]47人および中途で自害した[[萱野重実]]などを主祭神とする。主君の仇討ちという大願を果たした祭神に因み、「大願成就」の神徳で信仰を集める。 === 歴史 === 赤穂事件以降、赤穂浪士を称揚する人々によって旧[[赤穂城]]内の大石邸内に小さな祠が設けられ密かに祀られていたが、[[明治維新]]になり城売却と建物の破壊により耕地として払い下げられる。[[千種川]]（ちくさがわ）の洪水などもあり跡地は荒廃した。 [[1900年]]（[[明治]]33年）、あらためて「大石神社」として神社を創建することが政府から許可されたが、建築の募金が集まらず難航片山伯仙編「仙珪和尚日記抄」（花岳寺、1967年）、[[大町桂月]]ら右翼や国粋主義者桂月は[[与謝野晶子]]に対して「非国民」[乱臣賊子」と批判した事件で知られる。の反対もあった政教社「日本及日本人」（1909年）。[[1910年]]（明治43年）4月にようやく起工し、[[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）に社殿が竣工した。[[1928年]]（[[昭和]]3年）、無格社から[[県社]]に昇格された。 [[第二次世界大戦]]後、城内の神社に祀られていた[[赤穂藩]]主・[[浅野氏]]の3代（[[浅野長直|長直]]・[[浅野長友|長友]]・[[浅野長矩|長矩]]）および、城外の赤穂神社に祀られていた、浅野家の後に[[赤穂藩]]主となった[[森家]]の遠祖の七武将（[[森可成]]、[[森可隆]]、[[森長可]]、[[森成利]]（蘭丸）、[[森長隆]]（坊丸）、[[森長氏]]（力丸）、[[森忠政]]）を合祀した。 また、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列されている。[[2000年]]（平成12年）、赤穂神社と大石神社、および稲荷神社や恵比寿社などとの合祀により新たに'''赤穂大石神社'''として創祀。 === 高麗犬 === * 境内には 高麗犬（こまいぬ）の石像が随所に見られる。通常の神社における狛犬より獅子の要素が薄く犬に近い。 * 義士宝物殿には「犬の受け取り証」も展示されており[[脇坂氏|脇坂家]]文書「城内犬之覚」、[[浅野氏|浅野家]]治世の[[赤穂藩|赤穂]]が犬と非常に縁があった史料となっている。当社は[[犬]]を連れて参拝できる全国でも稀有な神社である。 === 境内 === 本殿、拝殿のほか、境内社として浅野家が藩主だった時代から藩民の崇敬を受けた摂末社が多数ある。宝物展示施設として、赤穂浪士の遺品などを陳列する義士宝物殿、長矩・四十七士らの木像を展示する義士木像奉安殿、大石邸長屋門（国の[[史跡]]「大石良雄宅跡」のうち）などがある。 * 本殿 - [[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）建立。 * 拝殿 - 1912年（大正元年）建立。 * 社務所 * 境内社合祀殿 - 国助[[稲荷社]]、[[淡島神社|淡嶋社]]、[[えびす|恵美寿宮]]、[[天満宮]]、山鹿社、八田社、忠魂社 * 義士史料館 - [[日本博物館協会]]会員館[https://www.j-muse.or.jp/introduction/detail/?mid=2942 大石神社義士史料館]公益財団法人日本博物館協会、[[兵庫県博物館協会]]加盟館[https://www2.hyogo-c.ed.jp/weblog2/museum-ac/?post_type=facility&user_prefix=007 エリア別施設一覧-西播磨]兵庫県博物館協会。[[全国歴史民俗系博物館協議会]]加盟館[https://rekimin.com/region/kinki/page/5 加盟館一覧-近畿]全国歴史民俗系博物館協議会。また[[ひょうごっ子ココロンカード]][https://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/syakai/kokoron/kokoron.html ひょうごっ子ココロンカード]兵庫県教育委員会の対象施設になっている。 ** 義士宝物殿 - [[大石良雄|大石内蔵助]]が所持していた[[長船派|備前長船]]清光・康光の大小刀、肖像掛軸など義士所縁の品々を展示。 ** 義士宝物殿別館 - [[浅野氏|浅野家]]・[[大石氏|大石家]]・[[森氏|森家]]の宝物を展示。 ** 義士木像奉安殿 - [[山崎朝雲]]、[[平櫛田中]]ら現代彫刻家による義士の彫像を展示。全49体。 ** 大石邸庭園（国指定[[史跡]]） ** 大石邸長屋門（国指定史跡） * 土蔵 * 大石稲荷社 * 神門 * 赤穂義士石造群 ファイル:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan01n.jpg|神門 ファイル:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan04bs3.jpg|義士宝物殿 ファイル:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan11n.jpg|大石邸長屋門・庭園 ファイル:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan13n.jpg|義士木像奉安殿と庭園 === 文化財 === ==== 国指定史跡 ==== * 大石良雄宅跡 - 管理団体は赤穂市。 === 祭典・年中行事 === * 1月15日 - [[とんど]]祭 * 4月第2日曜日 - 春の義士祭 * 5月5日 - 大石力餅 * 6月30日 - お田植祭 * 10月日曜日 - 抜穂祭 * 12月14日 - [[赤穂義士祭]] === 交通 === * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[赤穂線]] [[播州赤穂駅]]より徒歩15分。 === 外部リンク === * [http://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/ 赤穂大石神社]（公式サイト） * [http://www.city.ako.lg.jp/kensetsu/kankou/shisetsu/kanko/oishijinja.html 赤穂大石神社]（赤穂市のサイト） {{clear}} == 大石神社（京都） == {{神社 | 名称=大石神社 | 画像=[[ファイル:Kyoto Oishi Shrine haiden.jpeg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[京都市]][[山科区]]西野山桜ノ馬場町116 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|58|12|N|135|47|44|E|}} | 祭神=[[大石良雄|大石内蔵助良雄]] | 創建=[[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年） | 社格=旧[[府社]] | 例祭=[[4月14日]]（春季大祭）
[[12月14日]]（義士大祭） | 本殿=流造 | 地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=12|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=44|label=大石神社|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} }} [[京都市]][[山科区]]にある。旧社格は[[府社]]。[[大石良雄]]（内蔵助）を祀る。社地の付近は、[[元禄]]14年（[[1701年]]）7月から元禄15年（[[1702年]]）9月まで良雄が京都に隠棲し、義挙の議をめぐらしたときに居宅を構えた地である。主君の仇討ちという大願を果たした祭神に因み、「大願成就」の神徳で信仰を集める。 === 歴史 === [[昭和]]初期、赤穂浪士を熱心に崇拝していた浪曲師の吉田大和之丞（2代目[[吉田奈良丸]]）が、良雄ゆかりの地である[[岩屋寺 (京都市)|岩屋寺]]の北隣りに神社を創建することを計画した。府・市など関係方面に活動を行った結果、府知事を会長とする大石神社建設会などが設立され、募金によって[[1935年]]（昭和10年）に社殿が竣工した。 なお、創立が許可されたのは[[1933年]]（昭和8年）[[12月9日]]であった。また、[[1937年]]（昭和12年）4月に[[府社]]に列せられた。 === 境内 === * 本殿 - [[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年）建立。 * 拝殿 - 1935年（昭和10年）建立。 * 義人社 - 摂社。討入のための武器を納入したといわれる[[天野屋利兵衛]]を祀る。 * 社務所 * 宝物殿 === 小型馬 === * 境内では、[[ポニー|ミニホース]]の「花子」が[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）から飼育されている。 === 交通所要時間は、公式サイトの「交通のご案内」に準拠。 === * [[京阪バス]] 大石神社前[[バス停留所|バス停]]より徒歩1分。 === 外部リンク === * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ 大石神社]（公式サイト） [[ファイル:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|本殿|代替文=|左]] {{Clear}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * 黒糸素懸威伊予札丸胴、[[人間無骨]]（写し） - [[森長可]]所有。（[https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 人間無骨は、幕末に赤穂の刀匠・佐々木則之が鍛えた写し]） {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおいししんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:赤穂市の建築物]] [[Category:赤穂市の歴史]] [[Category:赤穂事件]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:山科区の建築物]] [[Category:山科区の歴史]] [[Category:兵庫県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:大石内蔵助]]

{{Otheruses}} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[shrine]] dedicated to [[Ōishi Yoshio]] and the [[Akō Rōnin]] (Akō Gishi), who carried out the raid in the [[Akō incident]]. During the [[Edo period]], open veneration was impossible due to restrictions by the [[Edo shogunate]]. However, in [[1868]] (the first year of the [[Meiji]] era), [[Emperor Meiji]] sent an imperial envoy to [[Sengaku-ji]], where the Akō Rōnin are buried, to pay tribute. This inspired movements to establish a shrine within Akō Castle. In the late [[Taishō]] and early [[Shōwa]] periods, shrines dedicated to the Akō Rōnin were established in [[Akō City]] and [[Kyōto City]]. It is one of the shrines that became popular in the late Edo period for venerating [[feudal lords]]. == Akō Ōishi Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Akō Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan02s3.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location=[[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Akō City]], Upper Kariya, Former Castle Grounds | coordinates={{Coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}} | deity=Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio and the 47 loyal retainers, [[Kayano Shigemitsu|Kayano Sanpei]], Three Generations of the Asano Clan, Seven Generals of the Mori Clan | Rank=Former [[Prefectural Shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | Established=[[1900]] (Meiji 33) | Pilgrimage Route=[[Sacred Sites of Kami and Buddhas Pilgrimage]] No. 76 (Hyōgo No. 11) | Festival=[[December 14]] ([[Akō Gishi Festival]]) | Map={{location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=57|lon_deg=134|lon_min=23|lon_sec=19|label=Akō Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}}}} }} {{Commonscat|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} Located in [[Akō Castle]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Akō City]], the shrine is a merger of Akō Shrine and Ōishi Shrine. Its former [[modern shrine Rank system|Rank]] was prefectural, and it is now listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The principal deities enshrined are the ancestral spirits of the [[Asano clan]] and [[Mori clan]], former [[Akō Domain]] lords, as well as [[Ōishi Yoshio]] and the 47 loyal retainers, including [[Kayano Shigemitsu]], who committed seppuku midway through the raid. As the shrine is associated with fulfilling the great wish of avenging a lord, it is believed to grant blessings for "great wish fulfillment." === History === After the Akō incident, followers of the Akō Rōnin secretly enshrined them in a small shrine within the former Ōishi residence in Akō Castle. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the castle was sold, and the buildings were demolished, leaving the site desolate due to floods from the [[Chikusa River]]. In [[1900]] (Meiji 33), the government authorized the establishment of "Ōishi Shrine," but fundraising efforts for construction faced difficulties.Katayama Hakusen, "Senkai Oshō Nikki-shō" (Kagakuji, 1967) Opposition also came from [[right-wing]] and [[nationalist]] figures such as {{ill|Ōmachi Keigetsu|ja|大町桂月|ca|Ōmachi Keigetsu|12=draft|12=draft}}.Seikyosha, "Nihon oyobi Nihonjin" (1909) Construction finally began in April [[1910]] (Meiji 43), and the shrine was completed in [[1912]] (Taishō 1). In [[1928]] (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to prefectural shrine status. After [[World War II]], the shrine merged with other shrines within and outside the castle grounds, including those dedicated to the Asano and Mori clans, as well as Inari Shrine and Ebisu Shrine, and was newly established as '''Akō Ōishi Shrine''' in [[2000]] (Heisei 12). === Korean Dogs === * The grounds feature statues of [[Komainu]] (Korean dogs), which are closer in appearance to dogs than the lion-like [[komainu]] typically found at other shrines. * The shrine houses historical artifacts such as a "Dog Receipt Certificate,"Wakisaka family documents: "Record of Dogs in the Castle Grounds" illustrating the historical connection between the Asano family and dogs in the Akō Domain. This shrine is one of the few in Japan that allows visitors to bring dogs. === Grounds === The shrine complex includes the main shrine, the worship hall, and several subordinate shrines. Facilities such as the Gishi Treasure Hall display artifacts related to the Akō Rōnin, while the Gishi Statue Hall exhibits wooden statues of the 47 retainers. * Main Hall – Built in [[1912]] (Taishō 1). * Worship Hall – Built in 1912. * Shrine Office * Subordinate Shrine Hall – Includes shrines such as [[Inari Shrine]], [[Awashima Shrine|Awashima Shrine]], and [[Tenmangū]]. * Gishi Historical Museum – A member of the [[Japan Museums Association]].[https://www.j-muse.or.jp/introduction/detail/?mid=2942 Akō Ōishi Shrine Gishi Historical Museum] Japan Museums Association Features a collection of relics related to the Akō Rōnin. * Gishi Statue Hall – Displays 49 sculptures of the loyal retainers by contemporary sculptors. === Cultural Properties === ==== National Historic Site ==== * Former Residence of Ōishi Yoshio – Managed by Akō City. === Festivals and Annual Events === * January 15 – [[Tondo]] Festival * April, 2nd Sunday – Spring Gishi Festival * May 5 – Ōishi Mochi Festival * June 30 – Rice Planting Festival * October, Sunday – Rice Harvesting Festival * December 14 – [[Akō Gishi Festival]] === Access === * 15-minute walk from [[JR West]]'s [[Akō Line]] [[Banshū-Akō Station]]. * 10-minute drive from [[Sanyō Expressway]] [[Akō Interchange]].Official shrine website, "History" section. === External Links === * [http://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/ Akō Ōishi Shrine] (official website) {{citation needed|Oishi Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine haiden.jpeg|270px]]
Worship Hall | location = 116 Nishinoyama Sakuranobabachō, Yamashina-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|58|12|N|135|47|44|E|}} | Deities = [[Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] | consecration_year = [[1935]] ([[Shōwa]] 10) | type = Former [[Fusha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | Main Festival = [[April 14]] (Spring Grand Festival), [[December 14]] (Gishi Festival) | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=12|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=44|label=Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamashina-ku]], [[Kyoto City]]. It enshrines [[Ōishi Yoshio]], also known as Ōishi Kuranosuke, the leader of the [[Akō Rōshi]] (47 Ronin). The shrine is situated near the location where Ōishi Yoshio lived in seclusion from July of [[Genroku]] 14 (1701) to September of Genroku 15 (1702) while planning the [[revenge]] of their lord. The shrine attracts worshippers who seek blessings for "great wish fulfillment" (大願成就), inspired by the determination of its enshrined deity. == History == In the early [[Shōwa]] era, a rōkyoku storyteller, Yoshida Yamato-no-Jō (second-generation [[Yoshida Narimaru]]), who deeply revered the Akō Rōshi, initiated the construction of a shrine dedicated to Ōishi Yoshio. With the support of local and governmental organizations, including the Governor of Kyoto serving as chair of the shrine construction committee, fundraising efforts led to the Completion tier 2]] (Shōwa 10). The shrine received permission for its establishment on [[December 9, 1933]] (Shōwa 8) and was designated as a [[Fusha]] in [[1937]] (Shōwa 12). == Features == * **Main Hall** - Built in [[1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Worship Hall** - Built in [[1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Gijinsya (Loyalty Shrine)** - A subordinate shrine enshrining [[Amano-ya Rihei]], who is said to have provided weapons for the assault on [[Kira Yoshinaka]]. * **Treasure Hall** - Houses artifacts and memorabilia associated with the Akō Rōshi. * **Administration Office** - Handles shrine operations. === Miniature Horse === The shrine keeps a miniature horse named "Hanako," which has been cared for within the precincts since [[2007]] (Heisei 19). == Festivals and Events == * **April 14** - Spring Grand Festival * **December 14** - Gishi Festival, commemorating the Akō Rōshi's raid. == Access == * 1-minute walk from the Ōishi Shrine-mae [[Keihan Bus]] stop. * 20 minutes by car from the [[Kyoto Higashi Interchange]] on the [[Meishin Expressway]]. * Close to [[Shinjūjō Street]] and [[Inariyama Tunnel]]. == External Links == * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ Official Website of Ōishi Shrine] [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|Main Hall|left]] {{Clear}} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * **Black Thread Armor** and [[Human Mukotsu]] replica - owned by [[Mori Nagayoshi]] ([https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 Human Mukotsu replica forged by Akō swordsmith Sasaki Noriyuki during the late Edo period]). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|Ōishi Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Akō City buildings]] [[Category:History of Akō City]] [[Category:Akō incident]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Oishi Shrine]] [[Category:forcing refresh for Confirmed wikipedia 11s 6]] [[Category: of the shrine buildings in [[1935]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture Former Fusha]] [[Category:Akō Incident]] [[Category:Ōishi Yoshio]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Oishi Shrine]] [[ja:大石神社]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Oishi Shrine]]

{{Otheruses}} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[shrine]] dedicated to [[Ōishi Yoshio]] and the [[Akō Rōnin]] (Akō Gishi), who carried out the raid in the [[Akō incident]]. During the [[Edo period]], open veneration was impossible due to restrictions by the [[Edo shogunate]]. However, in [[1868]] (the first year of the [[Meiji]] era), [[Emperor Meiji]] sent an imperial envoy to [[Sengaku-ji]], where the Akō Rōnin are buried, to pay tribute. This inspired movements to establish a shrine within Akō Castle. In the late [[Taishō]] and early [[Shōwa]] periods, shrines dedicated to the Akō Rōnin were established in [[Akō City]] and [[Kyōto City]]. It is one of the shrines that became popular in the late Edo period for venerating [[feudal lords]]. == Akō Ōishi Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Akō Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan02s3.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location=[[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Akō City]], Upper Kariya, Former Castle Grounds | coordinates={{Coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}} | deity=Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio and the 47 loyal retainers, [[Kayano Shigemitsu|Kayano Sanpei]], Three Generations of the Asano Clan, Seven Generals of the Mori Clan | Rank=Former [[Prefectural Shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | Established=[[1900]] (Meiji 33) | Pilgrimage Route=[[Sacred Sites of Kami and Buddhas Pilgrimage]] No. 76 (Hyōgo No. 11) | Festival=[[December 14]] ([[Akō Gishi Festival]]) | Map={{location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=57|lon_deg=134|lon_min=23|lon_sec=19|label=Akō Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}}}} }} {{Commonscat|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} Located in [[Akō Castle]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Akō City]], the shrine is a merger of Akō Shrine and Ōishi Shrine. Its former [[modern shrine Rank system|Rank]] was prefectural, and it is now listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The principal deities enshrined are the ancestral spirits of the [[Asano clan]] and [[Mori clan]], former [[Akō Domain]] lords, as well as [[Ōishi Yoshio]] and the 47 loyal retainers, including [[Kayano Shigemitsu]], who committed seppuku midway through the raid. As the shrine is associated with fulfilling the great wish of avenging a lord, it is believed to grant blessings for "great wish fulfillment." === History === After the Akō incident, followers of the Akō Rōnin secretly enshrined them in a small shrine within the former Ōishi residence in Akō Castle. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the castle was sold, and the buildings were demolished, leaving the site desolate due to floods from the [[Chikusa River]]. In [[1900]] (Meiji 33), the government authorized the establishment of "Ōishi Shrine," but fundraising efforts for construction faced difficulties.Katayama Hakusen, "Senkai Oshō Nikki-shō" (Kagakuji, 1967) Opposition also came from [[right-wing]] and [[nationalist]] figures such as {{ill|Ōmachi Keigetsu|ja|大町桂月|ca|Ōmachi Keigetsu}}.Seikyosha, "Nihon oyobi Nihonjin" (1909) Construction finally began in April [[1910]] (Meiji 43), and the shrine was completed in [[1912]] (Taishō 1). In [[1928]] (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to prefectural shrine status. After [[World War II]], the shrine merged with other shrines within and outside the castle grounds, including those dedicated to the Asano and Mori clans, as well as Inari Shrine and Ebisu Shrine, and was newly established as '''Akō Ōishi Shrine''' in [[2000]] (Heisei 12). === Korean Dogs === * The grounds feature statues of [[Komainu]] (Korean dogs), which are closer in appearance to dogs than the lion-like [[komainu]] typically found at other shrines. * The shrine houses historical artifacts such as a "Dog Receipt Certificate,"Wakisaka family documents: "Record of Dogs in the Castle Grounds" illustrating the historical connection between the Asano family and dogs in the Akō Domain. This shrine is one of the few in Japan that allows visitors to bring dogs. === Grounds === The shrine complex includes the main shrine, the worship hall, and several subordinate shrines. Facilities such as the Gishi Treasure Hall display artifacts related to the Akō Rōnin, while the Gishi Statue Hall exhibits wooden statues of the 47 retainers. * Main Hall – Built in [[1912]] (Taishō 1). * Worship Hall – Built in 1912. * Shrine Office * Subordinate Shrine Hall – Includes shrines such as [[Inari Shrine]], [[Awashima Shrine|Awashima Shrine]], and [[Tenmangū]]. * Gishi Historical Museum – A member of the [[Japan Museums Association]].[https://www.j-muse.or.jp/introduction/detail/?mid=2942 Akō Ōishi Shrine Gishi Historical Museum] Japan Museums Association Features a collection of relics related to the Akō Rōnin. * Gishi Statue Hall – Displays 49 sculptures of the loyal retainers by contemporary sculptors. === Cultural Properties === ==== National Historic Site ==== * Former Residence of Ōishi Yoshio – Managed by Akō City. === Festivals and Annual Events === * January 15 – [[Tondo]] Festival * April, 2nd Sunday – Spring Gishi Festival * May 5 – Ōishi Mochi Festival * June 30 – Rice Planting Festival * October, Sunday – Rice Harvesting Festival * December 14 – [[Akō Gishi Festival]] === Access === * 15-minute walk from [[JR West]]'s [[Akō Line]] [[Banshū-Akō Station]]. * 10-minute drive from [[Sanyō Expressway]] [[Akō Interchange]].Official shrine website, "History" section. === External Links === * [http://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/ Akō Ōishi Shrine] (official website) {{citation needed|Oishi Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine haiden.jpeg|270px]]
Worship Hall | location = 116 Nishinoyama Sakuranobabachō, Yamashina-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|58|12|N|135|47|44|E|}} | Deities = [[Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] | consecration_year = [[1935]] ([[Shōwa]] 10) | type = Former [[Fusha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | Main Festival = [[April 14]] (Spring Grand Festival), [[December 14]] (Gishi Festival) | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=12|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=44|label=Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamashina-ku]], [[Kyoto City]]. It enshrines [[Ōishi Yoshio]], also known as Ōishi Kuranosuke, the leader of the [[Akō Rōshi]] (47 Ronin). The shrine is situated near the location where Ōishi Yoshio lived in seclusion from July of [[Genroku]] 14 (1701) to September of Genroku 15 (1702) while planning the [[revenge]] of their lord. The shrine attracts worshippers who seek blessings for "great wish fulfillment" (大願成就), inspired by the determination of its enshrined deity. == History == In the early [[Shōwa]] era, a rōkyoku storyteller, Yoshida Yamato-no-Jō (second-generation [[Yoshida Narimaru]]), who deeply revered the Akō Rōshi, initiated the construction of a shrine dedicated to Ōishi Yoshio. With the support of local and governmental organizations, including the Governor of Kyoto serving as chair of the shrine construction committee, fundraising efforts led to the Completion tier 2]] (Shōwa 10). The shrine received permission for its establishment on [[December 9, 1933]] (Shōwa 8) and was designated as a [[Fusha]] in [[1937]] (Shōwa 12). == Features == * **Main Hall** - Built in [[1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Worship Hall** - Built in [[1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Gijinsya (Loyalty Shrine)** - A subordinate shrine enshrining [[Amano-ya Rihei]], who is said to have provided weapons for the assault on [[Kira Yoshinaka]]. * **Treasure Hall** - Houses artifacts and memorabilia associated with the Akō Rōshi. * **Administration Office** - Handles shrine operations. === Miniature Horse === The shrine keeps a miniature horse named "Hanako," which has been cared for within the precincts since [[2007]] (Heisei 19). == Festivals and Events == * **April 14** - Spring Grand Festival * **December 14** - Gishi Festival, commemorating the Akō Rōshi's raid. == Access == * 1-minute walk from the Ōishi Shrine-mae [[Keihan Bus]] stop. * 20 minutes by car from the [[Kyoto Higashi Interchange]] on the [[Meishin Expressway]]. * Close to [[Shinjūjō Street]] and [[Inariyama Tunnel]]. == External Links == * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ Official Website of Ōishi Shrine] [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|Main Hall|left]] {{Clear}} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * **Black Thread Armor** and [[Human Mukotsu]] replica - owned by [[Mori Nagayoshi]] ([https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 Human Mukotsu replica forged by Akō swordsmith Sasaki Noriyuki during the late Edo period]). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|Ōishi Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Akō City buildings]] [[Category:History of Akō City]] [[Category:Akō incident]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Oishi Shrine]] [[Category:forcing refresh for Confirmed wikipedia 11s 6]] [[Category: of the shrine buildings in [[1935]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture Former Fusha]] [[Category:Akō Incident]] [[Category:Ōishi Yoshio]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Oishi Shrine]] [[ja:大石神社]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Oishi Shrine]]

After [[World War II]], the shrine merged with other shrines within and outside the castle grounds, including those dedicated to the Asano and Mori clans, as well as Inari Shrine and Ebisu Shrine, and was newly established as '''Akō Ōishi Shrine''' in [[2000]] (Heisei 12). === Korean Dogs === * The grounds feature statues of [[Komainu]] (Korean dogs), which are closer in appearance to dogs than the lion-like [[komainu]] typically found at other shrines. * The shrine houses historical artifacts such as a "Dog Receipt Certificate,"Wakisaka family documents: "Record of Dogs in the Castle Grounds" illustrating the historical connection between the Asano family and dogs in the Akō Domain. This shrine is one of the few in Japan that allows visitors to bring dogs. === Grounds === The shrine complex includes the main shrine, the worship hall, and several subordinate shrines. Facilities such as the Gishi Treasure Hall display artifacts related to the Akō Rōnin, while the Gishi Statue Hall exhibits wooden statues of the 47 retainers. * Main Hall – Built in [[1912]] (Taishō 1). * Worship Hall – Built in 1912. * Shrine Office * Subordinate Shrine Hall – Includes shrines such as [[Inari Shrine]], [[Awashima Shrine|Awashima Shrine]], and [[Tenmangū]]. * Gishi Historical Museum – A member of the [[Japan Museums Association]].[https://www.j-muse.or.jp/introduction/detail/?mid=2942 Akō Ōishi Shrine Gishi Historical Museum] Japan Museums Association Features a collection of relics related to the Akō Rōnin. * Gishi Statue Hall – Displays 49 sculptures of the loyal retainers by contemporary sculptors. === Cultural Properties === ==== National Historic Site ==== * Former Residence of Ōishi Yoshio – Managed by Akō City. === Festivals and Annual Events === * January 15 – [[Tondo]] Festival * April, 2nd Sunday – Spring Gishi Festival * May 5 – Ōishi Mochi Festival * June 30 – Rice Planting Festival * October, Sunday – Rice Harvesting Festival * December 14 – [[Akō Gishi Festival]] === Access === * 15-minute walk from [[JR West]]'s [[Akō Line]] [[Banshū-Akō Station]]. * 10-minute drive from [[Sanyō Expressway]] [[Akō Interchange]].Official shrine website, "History" section. === External Links === * [http://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/ Akō Ōishi Shrine] (official website) {{citation needed|Oishi Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine haiden.jpeg|270px]]
Worship Hall | location = 116 Nishinoyama Sakuranobabachō, Yamashina-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|58|12|N|135|47|44|E|}} | Deities = [[Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] | consecration_year = [[1935]] ([[Shōwa]] 10) | type = Former [[Fusha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | Main Festival = [[April 14]] (Spring Grand Festival), [[December 14]] (Gishi Festival) | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=12|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=44|label=Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamashina-ku]], [[Kyoto City]]. It enshrines [[Ōishi Yoshio]], also known as Ōishi Kuranosuke, the leader of the [[Akō Rōshi]] (47 Ronin). The shrine is situated near the location where Ōishi Yoshio lived in seclusion from July of [[Genroku]] 14 (1701) to September of Genroku 15 (1702) while planning the [[revenge]] of their lord. The shrine attracts worshippers who seek blessings for "great wish fulfillment" (大願成就), inspired by the determination of its enshrined deity. == History == In the early [[Shōwa]] era, a rōkyoku storyteller, Yoshida Yamato-no-Jō (second-generation [[Yoshida Narimaru]]), who deeply revered the Akō Rōshi, initiated the construction of a shrine dedicated to Ōishi Yoshio. With the support of local and governmental organizations, including the Governor of Kyoto serving as chair of the shrine construction committee, fundraising efforts led to the Completion tier 2]] (Shōwa 10). The shrine received permission for its establishment on [[December 9, 1933]] (Shōwa 8) and was designated as a [[Fusha]] in [[1937]] (Shōwa 12). == Features == * **Main Hall** - Built in [[1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Worship Hall** - Built in [[1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Gijinsya (Loyalty Shrine)** - A subordinate shrine enshrining [[Amano-ya Rihei]], who is said to have provided weapons for the assault on [[Kira Yoshinaka]]. * **Treasure Hall** - Houses artifacts and memorabilia associated with the Akō Rōshi. * **Administration Office** - Handles shrine operations. === Miniature Horse === The shrine keeps a miniature horse named "Hanako," which has been cared for within the precincts since [[2007]] (Heisei 19). == Festivals and Events == * **April 14** - Spring Grand Festival * **December 14** - Gishi Festival, commemorating the Akō Rōshi's raid. == Access == * 1-minute walk from the Ōishi Shrine-mae [[Keihan Bus]] stop. * 20 minutes by car from the [[Kyoto Higashi Interchange]] on the [[Meishin Expressway]]. * Close to [[Shinjūjō Street]] and [[Inariyama Tunnel]]. == External Links == * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ Official Website of Ōishi Shrine] [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|Main Hall|left]] {{Clear}} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * **Black Thread Armor** and [[Human Mukotsu]] replica - owned by [[Mori Nagayoshi]] ([https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 Human Mukotsu replica forged by Akō swordsmith Sasaki Noriyuki during the late Edo period]). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|Ōishi Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Akō City buildings]] [[Category:History of Akō City]] [[Category:Akō incident]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Oishi Shrine]] [[Category:forcing refresh for Confirmed wikipedia 11s 6]] [[Category: of the shrine buildings in [[1935]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture Former Fusha]] [[Category:Akō Incident]] [[Category:Ōishi Yoshio]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]] }} [[sn:Oishi Shrine]] [[ja:大石神社]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Oishi Shrine]]

revision.comment: 75 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大石神社 up to rev 104543748
revision.text:
{{Otheruses}} '''大石神社'''（おおいしじんじゃ）は、[[赤穂事件]]において討ち入りをした[[大石良雄]]ら[[赤穂事件の人物一覧|赤穂浪士]]（赤穂義士）を祀る[[神社]]。[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]にはばかって表立って顕彰することはできなかったが、[[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年）、[[明治天皇]]が赤穂浪士の墓のある[[泉岳寺]]に勅使を遣わし、これを弔って以降、赤穂城内に神社を作ろうとする動きが起きた。大正から昭和初期に[[赤穂市]]と[[京都市]]に赤穂浪士を祀る神社が創建された。江戸時代後期から流行していた[[藩祖]]を祀った神社のひとつ。 == 赤穂大石神社 == {{神社 | 名称=赤穂大石神社 | 画像=[[ファイル:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan02s3.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[兵庫県]][[赤穂市]]上仮屋旧城内 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E|}} | 祭神=大石内蔵助良雄以下四十七士命、[[萱野重実|萱野三平]]
浅野家三代
森家七将 | 社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[1900年]]（[[明治]]33年） | 札所等=[[神仏霊場巡拝の道]]第76番（兵庫第11番） | 例祭=[[12月14日]]（[[赤穂義士祭]]） | 地図={{Location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=57|lon_deg=134|lon_min=23|lon_sec=19|label=赤穂大石神社|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}}}} }} {{Commonscat|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[兵庫県]][[赤穂市]]の[[赤穂城]]の中にある。赤穂神社と大石神社が統合されたもの。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は県社で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。旧・赤穂神社祭神の[[赤穂藩]]主[[浅野氏|浅野家]]・[[森氏|森家]]祖霊と旧・大石神社祭神の[[大石良雄]]ら[[赤穂事件の人物一覧|赤穂浪士]]47人および中途で自害した[[萱野重実]]などを主祭神とする。主君の仇討ちという大願を果たした祭神に因み、「大願成就」の神徳で信仰を集める。 === 歴史 === 赤穂事件以降、赤穂浪士を称揚する人々によって旧[[赤穂城]]内の大石邸内に小さな祠が設けられ密かに祀られていたが、[[明治維新]]になり城売却と建物の破壊により耕地として払い下げられる。[[千種川]]（ちくさがわ）の洪水などもあり跡地は荒廃した。 [[1900年]]（[[明治]]33年）、あらためて「大石神社」として神社を創建することが政府から許可されたが、建築の募金が集まらず難航片山伯仙編「仙珪和尚日記抄」（花岳寺、1967年）、[[大町桂月]]ら右翼や国粋主義者桂月は[[与謝野晶子]]に対して「非国民」[乱臣賊子」と批判した事件で知られる。の反対もあった政教社「日本及日本人」（1909年）。[[1910年]]（明治43年）4月にようやく起工し、[[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）に社殿が竣工した。[[1928年]]（[[昭和]]3年）、無格社から[[県社]]に昇格された。 [[第二次世界大戦]]後、城内の神社に祀られていた[[赤穂藩]]主・[[浅野氏]]の3代（[[浅野長直|長直]]・[[浅野長友|長友]]・[[浅野長矩|長矩]]）および、城外の赤穂神社に祀られていた、浅野家の後に[[赤穂藩]]主となった[[森家]]の遠祖の七武将（[[森可成]]、[[森可隆]]、[[森長可]]、[[森成利]]（蘭丸）、[[森長隆]]（坊丸）、[[森長氏]]（力丸）、[[森忠政]]）を合祀した。 また、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列されている。[[2000年]]（平成12年）、赤穂神社と大石神社、および稲荷神社や恵比寿社などとの合祀により新たに'''赤穂大石神社'''として創祀。 === 高麗犬 === * 境内には 高麗犬（こまいぬ）の石像が随所に見られる。通常の神社における狛犬より獅子の要素が薄く犬に近い。 * 義士宝物殿には「犬の受け取り証」も展示されており[[脇坂氏|脇坂家]]文書「城内犬之覚」、[[浅野氏|浅野家]]治世の[[赤穂藩|赤穂]]が犬と非常に縁があった史料となっている。当社は[[犬]]を連れて参拝できる全国でも稀有な神社である。 === 境内 === 本殿、拝殿のほか、境内社として浅野家が藩主だった時代から藩民の崇敬を受けた摂末社が多数ある。宝物展示施設として、赤穂浪士の遺品などを陳列する義士宝物殿、長矩・四十七士らの木像を展示する義士木像奉安殿、大石邸長屋門（国の[[史跡]]「大石良雄宅跡」のうち）などがある。 * 本殿 - [[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）建立。 * 拝殿 - 1912年（大正元年）建立。 * 社務所 * 境内社合祀殿 - 国助[[稲荷社]]、[[淡島神社|淡嶋社]]、[[えびす|恵美寿宮]]、[[天満宮]]、山鹿社、八田社、忠魂社 * 義士史料館 - [[日本博物館協会]]会員館[https://www.j-muse.or.jp/introduction/detail/?mid=2942 大石神社義士史料館]公益財団法人日本博物館協会、[[兵庫県博物館協会]]加盟館[https://www2.hyogo-c.ed.jp/weblog2/museum-ac/?post_type=facility&user_prefix=007 エリア別施設一覧-西播磨]兵庫県博物館協会。[[全国歴史民俗系博物館協議会]]加盟館[https://rekimin.com/region/kinki/page/5 加盟館一覧-近畿]全国歴史民俗系博物館協議会。また[[ひょうごっ子ココロンカード]][https://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/syakai/kokoron/kokoron.html ひょうごっ子ココロンカード]兵庫県教育委員会の対象施設になっている。 ** 義士宝物殿 - [[大石良雄|大石内蔵助]]が所持していた[[長船派|備前長船]]清光・康光の大小刀、肖像掛軸など義士所縁の品々を展示。 ** 義士宝物殿別館 - [[浅野氏|浅野家]]・[[大石氏|大石家]]・[[森氏|森家]]の宝物を展示。 ** 義士木像奉安殿 - [[山崎朝雲]]、[[平櫛田中]]ら現代彫刻家による義士の彫像を展示。全49体。 ** 大石邸庭園（国指定[[史跡]]） ** 大石邸長屋門（国指定史跡） * 土蔵 * 大石稲荷社 * 神門 * 赤穂義士石造群 ファイル:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan01n.jpg|神門 ファイル:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan04bs3.jpg|義士宝物殿 ファイル:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan11n.jpg|大石邸長屋門・庭園 ファイル:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan13n.jpg|義士木像奉安殿と庭園 === 文化財 === ==== 国指定史跡 ==== * 大石良雄宅跡 - 管理団体は赤穂市。 === 祭典・年中行事 === * 1月15日 - [[とんど]]祭 * 4月第2日曜日 - 春の義士祭 * 5月5日 - 大石力餅 * 6月30日 - お田植祭 * 10月日曜日 - 抜穂祭 * 12月14日 - [[赤穂義士祭]] === 交通 === * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[赤穂線]] [[播州赤穂駅]]より徒歩15分。 === 外部リンク === * [http://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/ 赤穂大石神社]（公式サイト） * [http://www.city.ako.lg.jp/kensetsu/kankou/shisetsu/kanko/oishijinja.html 赤穂大石神社]（赤穂市のサイト） {{clear}} == 大石神社（京都） == {{神社 | 名称=大石神社 | 画像=[[ファイル:Kyoto Oishi Shrine haiden.jpeg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[京都市]][[山科区]]西野山桜ノ馬場町116 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|58|12|N|135|47|44|E|}} | 祭神=[[大石良雄|大石内蔵助良雄]] | 創建=[[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年） | 社格=旧[[府社]] | 例祭=[[4月14日]]（春季大祭）
[[12月14日]]（義士大祭） | 本殿=流造 | 地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=12|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=44|label=大石神社|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} }} [[京都市]][[山科区]]にある。旧社格は[[府社]]。[[大石良雄]]（内蔵助）を祀る。社地の付近は、[[元禄]]14年（[[1701年]]）7月から元禄15年（[[1702年]]）9月まで良雄が京都に隠棲し、義挙の議をめぐらしたときに居宅を構えた地である。主君の仇討ちという大願を果たした祭神に因み、「大願成就」の神徳で信仰を集める。 === 歴史 === [[昭和]]初期、赤穂浪士を熱心に崇拝していた浪曲師の吉田大和之丞（2代目[[吉田奈良丸]]）が、良雄ゆかりの地である[[岩屋寺 (京都市)|岩屋寺]]の北隣りに神社を創建することを計画した。府・市など関係方面に活動を行った結果、府知事を会長とする大石神社建設会などが設立され、募金によって[[1935年]]（昭和10年）に社殿が竣工した。 なお、創立が許可されたのは[[1933年]]（昭和8年）[[12月9日]]であった。また、[[1937年]]（昭和12年）4月に[[府社]]に列せられた。 === 境内 === * 本殿 - [[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年）建立。 * 拝殿 - 1935年（昭和10年）建立。 * 義人社 - 摂社。討入のための武器を納入したといわれる[[天野屋利兵衛]]を祀る。 * 社務所 * 宝物殿 === 小型馬 === * 境内では、[[ポニー|ミニホース]]の「花子」が[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）から飼育されている。 === 交通所要時間は、公式サイトの「交通のご案内」に準拠。 === * [[京阪バス]] 大石神社前[[バス停留所|バス停]]より徒歩1分。 === 外部リンク === * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ 大石神社]（公式サイト） [[ファイル:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|本殿|代替文=|左]] {{Clear}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * 黒糸素懸威伊予札丸胴、[[人間無骨]]（写し） - [[森長可]]所有。（[https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 人間無骨は、幕末に赤穂の刀匠・佐々木則之が鍛えた写し]） {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおいししんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:赤穂市の建築物]] [[Category:赤穂市の歴史]] [[Category:赤穂事件]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:山科区の建築物]] [[Category:山科区の歴史]] [[Category:兵庫県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:大石内蔵助]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses}} '''大石神社'''（おおいしじんじゃ）は、[[赤穂事件]]において討ち入りをした[[大石良雄]]ら[[赤穂事件の人物一覧|赤穂浪士]]（赤穂義士）を祀る[[神社]]。[[江戸時代]]には[[江戸幕府]]にはばかって表立って顕彰することはできなかったが、[[1868年]]（[[明治]]元年）、[[明治天皇]]が赤穂浪士の墓のある[[泉岳寺]]に勅使を遣わし、これを弔って以降、赤穂城内に神社を作ろうとする動きが起きた。大正から昭和初期に[[赤穂市]]と[[京都市]]に赤穂浪士を祀る神社が創建された。江戸時代後期から流行していた[[藩祖]]を祀った神社のひとつ。 == 赤穂大石神社 == {{神社 | 名称=赤穂大石神社 | 画像=[[ファイル:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan02s3.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[兵庫県]][[赤穂市]]上仮屋旧城内 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E|}} | 祭神=大石内蔵助良雄以下四十七士命、[[萱野重実|萱野三平]]
浅野家三代
森家七将 | 社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[1900年]]（[[明治]]33年） | 札所等=[[神仏霊場巡拝の道]]第76番（兵庫第11番） | 例祭=[[12月14日]]（[[赤穂義士祭]]） | 地図={{Location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=57|lon_deg=134|lon_min=23|lon_sec=19|label=赤穂大石神社|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}}}} }} {{Commonscat|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[兵庫県]][[赤穂市]]の[[赤穂城]]の中にある。赤穂神社と大石神社が統合されたもの。旧[[近代社格制度|社格]]は県社で、現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。旧・赤穂神社祭神の[[赤穂藩]]主[[浅野氏|浅野家]]・[[森氏|森家]]祖霊と旧・大石神社祭神の[[大石良雄]]ら[[赤穂事件の人物一覧|赤穂浪士]]47人および中途で自害した[[萱野重実]]などを主祭神とする。主君の仇討ちという大願を果たした祭神に因み、「大願成就」の神徳で信仰を集める。 === 歴史 === 赤穂事件以降、赤穂浪士を称揚する人々によって旧[[赤穂城]]内の大石邸内に小さな祠が設けられ密かに祀られていたが、[[明治維新]]になり城売却と建物の破壊により耕地として払い下げられる。[[千種川]]（ちくさがわ）の洪水などもあり跡地は荒廃した。 [[1900年]]（[[明治]]33年）、あらためて「大石神社」として神社を創建することが政府から許可されたが、建築の募金が集まらず難航片山伯仙編「仙珪和尚日記抄」（花岳寺、1967年）、[[大町桂月]]ら右翼や国粋主義者桂月は[[与謝野晶子]]に対して「非国民」[乱臣賊子」と批判した事件で知られる。の反対もあった政教社「日本及日本人」（1909年）。[[1910年]]（明治43年）4月にようやく起工し、[[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）に社殿が竣工した。[[1928年]]（[[昭和]]3年）、無格社から[[県社]]に昇格された。 [[第二次世界大戦]]後、城内の神社に祀られていた[[赤穂藩]]主・[[浅野氏]]の3代（[[浅野長直|長直]]・[[浅野長友|長友]]・[[浅野長矩|長矩]]）および、城外の赤穂神社に祀られていた、浅野家の後に[[赤穂藩]]主となった[[森家]]の遠祖の七武将（[[森可成]]、[[森可隆]]、[[森長可]]、[[森成利]]（蘭丸）、[[森長隆]]（坊丸）、[[森長氏]]（力丸）、[[森忠政]]）を合祀した。 また、[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]に加列されている。[[2000年]]（平成12年）、赤穂神社と大石神社、および稲荷神社や恵比寿社などとの合祀により新たに'''赤穂大石神社'''として創祀。 === 高麗犬 === * 境内には 高麗犬（こまいぬ）の石像が随所に見られる。通常の神社における狛犬より獅子の要素が薄く犬に近い。 * 義士宝物殿には「犬の受け取り証」も展示されており[[脇坂氏|脇坂家]]文書「城内犬之覚」、[[浅野氏|浅野家]]治世の[[赤穂藩|赤穂]]が犬と非常に縁があった史料となっている。当社は[[犬]]を連れて参拝できる全国でも稀有な神社である。 === 境内 === 本殿、拝殿のほか、境内社として浅野家が藩主だった時代から藩民の崇敬を受けた摂末社が多数ある。宝物展示施設として、赤穂浪士の遺品などを陳列する義士宝物殿、長矩・四十七士らの木像を展示する義士木像奉安殿、大石邸長屋門（国の[[史跡]]「大石良雄宅跡」のうち）などがある。 * 本殿 - [[1912年]]（[[大正]]元年）建立。 * 拝殿 - 1912年（大正元年）建立。 * 社務所 * 境内社合祀殿 - 国助[[稲荷社]]、[[淡島神社|淡嶋社]]、[[えびす|恵美寿宮]]、[[天満宮]]、山鹿社、八田社、忠魂社 * 義士史料館 - [[日本博物館協会]]会員館[https://www.j-muse.or.jp/introduction/detail/?mid=2942 大石神社義士史料館]公益財団法人日本博物館協会、[[兵庫県博物館協会]]加盟館[https://www2.hyogo-c.ed.jp/weblog2/museum-ac/?post_type=facility&user_prefix=007 エリア別施設一覧-西播磨]兵庫県博物館協会。[[全国歴史民俗系博物館協議会]]加盟館[https://rekimin.com/region/kinki/page/5 加盟館一覧-近畿]全国歴史民俗系博物館協議会。また[[ひょうごっ子ココロンカード]][https://www.hyogo-c.ed.jp/~shabun-bo/syakai/kokoron/kokoron.html ひょうごっ子ココロンカード]兵庫県教育委員会の対象施設になっている。 ** 義士宝物殿 - [[大石良雄|大石内蔵助]]が所持していた[[長船派|備前長船]]清光・康光の大小刀、肖像掛軸など義士所縁の品々を展示。 ** 義士宝物殿別館 - [[浅野氏|浅野家]]・[[大石氏|大石家]]・[[森氏|森家]]の宝物を展示。 ** 義士木像奉安殿 - [[山崎朝雲]]、[[平櫛田中]]ら現代彫刻家による義士の彫像を展示。全49体。 ** 大石邸庭園（国指定[[史跡]]） ** 大石邸長屋門（国指定史跡） * 土蔵 * 大石稲荷社 * 神門 * 赤穂義士石造群 ファイル:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan01n.jpg|神門 ファイル:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan04bs3.jpg|義士宝物殿 ファイル:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan11n.jpg|大石邸長屋門・庭園 ファイル:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan13n.jpg|義士木像奉安殿と庭園 === 文化財 === ==== 国指定史跡 ==== * 大石良雄宅跡 - 管理団体は赤穂市。 === 祭典・年中行事 === * 1月15日 - [[とんど]]祭 * 4月第2日曜日 - 春の義士祭 * 5月5日 - 大石力餅 * 6月30日 - お田植祭 * 10月日曜日 - 抜穂祭 * 12月14日 - [[赤穂義士祭]] === 交通 === * [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]][[赤穂線]] [[播州赤穂駅]]より徒歩15分。 === 外部リンク === * [http://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/ 赤穂大石神社]（公式サイト） * [http://www.city.ako.lg.jp/kensetsu/kankou/shisetsu/kanko/oishijinja.html 赤穂大石神社]（赤穂市のサイト） {{clear}} == 大石神社（京都） == {{神社 | 名称=大石神社 | 画像=[[ファイル:Kyoto Oishi Shrine haiden.jpeg|270px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[京都市]][[山科区]]西野山桜ノ馬場町116 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|34|58|12|N|135|47|44|E|}} | 祭神=[[大石良雄|大石内蔵助良雄]] | 創建=[[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年） | 社格=旧[[府社]] | 例祭=[[4月14日]]（春季大祭）
[[12月14日]]（義士大祭） | 本殿=流造 | 地図={{Location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=12|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=44|label=大石神社|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} }} [[京都市]][[山科区]]にある。旧社格は[[府社]]。[[大石良雄]]（内蔵助）を祀る。社地の付近は、[[元禄]]14年（[[1701年]]）7月から元禄15年（[[1702年]]）9月まで良雄が京都に隠棲し、義挙の議をめぐらしたときに居宅を構えた地である。主君の仇討ちという大願を果たした祭神に因み、「大願成就」の神徳で信仰を集める。 === 歴史 === [[昭和]]初期、赤穂浪士を熱心に崇拝していた浪曲師の吉田大和之丞（2代目[[吉田奈良丸]]）が、良雄ゆかりの地である[[岩屋寺 (京都市)|岩屋寺]]の北隣りに神社を創建することを計画した。府・市など関係方面に活動を行った結果、府知事を会長とする大石神社建設会などが設立され、募金によって[[1935年]]（昭和10年）に社殿が竣工した。 なお、創立が許可されたのは[[1933年]]（昭和8年）[[12月9日]]であった。また、[[1937年]]（昭和12年）4月に[[府社]]に列せられた。 === 境内 === * 本殿 - [[1935年]]（[[昭和]]10年）建立。 * 拝殿 - 1935年（昭和10年）建立。 * 義人社 - 摂社。討入のための武器を納入したといわれる[[天野屋利兵衛]]を祀る。 * 社務所 * 宝物殿 === 小型馬 === * 境内では、[[ポニー|ミニホース]]の「花子」が[[平成]]19年（[[2007年]]）から飼育されている。 === 交通所要時間は、公式サイトの「交通のご案内」に準拠。 === * [[京阪バス]] 大石神社前[[バス停留所|バス停]]より徒歩1分。 === 外部リンク === * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ 大石神社]（公式サイト） [[ファイル:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|本殿|代替文=|左]] {{Clear}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == 関連項目 == * 黒糸素懸威伊予札丸胴、[[人間無骨]]（写し） - [[森長可]]所有。（[https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 人間無骨は、幕末に赤穂の刀匠・佐々木則之が鍛えた写し]） {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおいししんしや}} [[Category:兵庫県の神社]] [[Category:赤穂市の建築物]] [[Category:赤穂市の歴史]] [[Category:赤穂事件]] [[Category:京都市の神社]] [[Category:山科区の建築物]] [[Category:山科区の歴史]] [[Category:兵庫県にある国指定の史跡]] [[Category:兵庫県の旧県社]] [[Category:京都府の旧府社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:大石内蔵助]]

{{Otheruses}} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[shrine]] dedicated to [[Ōishi Yoshio]] and the [[Akō Rōnin]] (Akō Gishi), who carried out the raid in the [[Akō incident]]. During the [[Edo period]], open veneration was impossible due to restrictions by the [[Edo shogunate]]. However, in [[1868]] (the first year of the [[Meiji]] era), [[Emperor Meiji]] sent an imperial envoy to [[Sengaku-ji]], where the Akō Rōnin are buried, to pay tribute. This inspired movements to establish a shrine within Akō Castle. In the late [[Taishō]] and early [[Shōwa]] periods, shrines dedicated to the Akō Rōnin were established in [[Akō City]] and [[Kyōto City]]. It is one of the shrines that became popular in the late Edo period for venerating [[feudal lords]]. == Akō Ōishi Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Akō Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan02s3.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location=[[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Akō City]], Upper Kariya, Former Castle Grounds | coordinates={{Coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}} | deity=Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio and the 47 loyal retainers, [[Kayano Shigemitsu|Kayano Sanpei]], Three Generations of the Asano Clan, Seven Generals of the Mori Clan | Rank=Former [[Prefectural Shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | Established=[[1900]] (Meiji 33) | Pilgrimage Route=[[Sacred Sites of Kami and Buddhas Pilgrimage]] No. 76 (Hyōgo No. 11) | Festival=[[December 14]] ([[Akō Gishi Festival]]) | Map={{location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=57|lon_deg=134|lon_min=23|lon_sec=19|label=Akō Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}}}} }} {{Commonscat|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} Located in [[Akō Castle]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Akō City]], the shrine is a merger of Akō Shrine and Ōishi Shrine. Its former [[modern shrine Rank system|Rank]] was prefectural, and it is now listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The principal deities enshrined are the ancestral spirits of the [[Asano clan]] and [[Mori clan]], former [[Akō Domain]] lords, as well as [[Ōishi Yoshio]] and the 47 loyal retainers, including [[Kayano Shigemitsu]], who committed seppuku midway through the raid. As the shrine is associated with fulfilling the great wish of avenging a lord, it is believed to grant blessings for "great wish fulfillment." === History === After the Akō incident, followers of the Akō Rōnin secretly enshrined them in a small shrine within the former Ōishi residence in Akō Castle. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the castle was sold, and the buildings were demolished, leaving the site desolate due to floods from the [[Chikusa River]]. In [[1900]] (Meiji 33), the government authorized the establishment of "Ōishi Shrine," but fundraising efforts for construction faced difficulties.Katayama Hakusen, "Senkai Oshō Nikki-shō" (Kagakuji, 1967) Opposition also came from [[right-wing]] and [[nationalist]] figures such as {{ill|Ōmachi Keigetsu|ja|大町桂月|ca|Ōmachi Keigetsu}}.Seikyosha, "Nihon oyobi Nihonjin" (1909) Construction finally began in April [[1910]] (Meiji 43), and the shrine was completed in [[1912]] (Taishō 1). In [[1928]] (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to prefectural shrine status. After [[World War II]], the shrine merged with other shrines within and outside the castle grounds, including those dedicated to the Asano and Mori clans, as well as Inari Shrine and Ebisu Shrine, and was newly established as '''Akō Ōishi Shrine''' in [[2000]] (Heisei 12). === Korean Dogs === * The grounds feature statues of [[Komainu]] (Korean dogs), which are closer in appearance to dogs than the lion-like [[komainu]] typically found at other shrines. * The shrine houses historical artifacts such as a "Dog Receipt Certificate,"Wakisaka family documents: "Record of Dogs in the Castle Grounds" illustrating the historical connection between the Asano family and dogs in the Akō Domain. This shrine is one of the few in Japan that allows visitors to bring dogs. === Grounds === The shrine complex includes the main shrine, the worship hall, and several subordinate shrines. Facilities such as the Gishi Treasure Hall display artifacts related to the Akō Rōnin, while the Gishi Statue Hall exhibits wooden statues of the 47 retainers. * Main Hall – Built in [[1912]] (Taishō 1). * Worship Hall – Built in 1912. * Shrine Office * Subordinate Shrine Hall – Includes shrines such as [[Inari Shrine]], [[Awashima Shrine|Awashima Shrine]], and [[Tenmangū]]. * Gishi Historical Museum – A member of the [[Japan Museums Association]].[https://www.j-muse.or.jp/introduction/detail/?mid=2942 Akō Ōishi Shrine Gishi Historical Museum] Japan Museums Association Features a collection of relics related to the Akō Rōnin. * Gishi Statue Hall – Displays 49 sculptures of the loyal retainers by contemporary sculptors. === Cultural Properties === ==== National Historic Site ==== * Former Residence of Ōishi Yoshio – Managed by Akō City. === Festivals and Annual Events === * January 15 – [[Tondo]] Festival * April, 2nd Sunday – Spring Gishi Festival * May 5 – Ōishi Mochi Festival * June 30 – Rice Planting Festival * October, Sunday – Rice Harvesting Festival * December 14 – [[Akō Gishi Festival]] === Access === * 15-minute walk from [[JR West]]'s [[Akō Line]] [[Banshū-Akō Station]]. * 10-minute drive from [[Sanyō Expressway]] [[Akō Interchange]].Official shrine website, "History" section. === External Links === * [http://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/ Akō Ōishi Shrine] (official website) {{citation needed|Oishi Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine haiden.jpeg|270px]]
Worship Hall | location = 116 Nishinoyama Sakuranobabachō, Yamashina-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|58|12|N|135|47|44|E|}} | Deities = [[Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] | consecration_year = [[1935]] ([[Shōwa]] 10) | type = Former [[Fusha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | Main Festival = [[April 14]] (Spring Grand Festival), [[December 14]] (Gishi Festival) | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=12|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=44|label=Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamashina-ku]], [[Kyoto City]]. It enshrines [[Ōishi Yoshio]], also known as Ōishi Kuranosuke, the leader of the [[Akō Rōshi]] (47 Ronin). The shrine is situated near the location where Ōishi Yoshio lived in seclusion from July of [[Genroku]] 14 (1701) to September of Genroku 15 (1702) while planning the [[revenge]] of their lord. The shrine attracts worshippers who seek blessings for "great wish fulfillment" (大願成就), inspired by the determination of its enshrined deity. == History == In the early [[Shōwa]] era, a rōkyoku storyteller, Yoshida Yamato-no-Jō (second-generation [[Yoshida Narimaru]]), who deeply revered the Akō Rōshi, initiated the construction of a shrine dedicated to Ōishi Yoshio. With the support of local and governmental organizations, including the Governor of Kyoto serving as chair of the shrine construction committee, fundraising efforts led to the Completion tier 2]] (Shōwa 10). The shrine received permission for its establishment on [[December 9, 1933]] (Shōwa 8) and was designated as a [[Fusha]] in [[1937]] (Shōwa 12). == Features == * **Main Hall** - Built in [[1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Worship Hall** - Built in [[1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Gijinsya (Loyalty Shrine)** - A subordinate shrine enshrining [[Amano-ya Rihei]], who is said to have provided weapons for the assault on [[Kira Yoshinaka]]. * **Treasure Hall** - Houses artifacts and memorabilia associated with the Akō Rōshi. * **Administration Office** - Handles shrine operations. === Miniature Horse === The shrine keeps a miniature horse named "Hanako," which has been cared for within the precincts since [[2007]] (Heisei 19). == Festivals and Events == * **April 14** - Spring Grand Festival * **December 14** - Gishi Festival, commemorating the Akō Rōshi's raid. == Access == * 1-minute walk from the Ōishi Shrine-mae [[Keihan Bus]] stop. * 20 minutes by car from the [[Kyoto Higashi Interchange]] on the [[Meishin Expressway]]. * Close to [[Shinjūjō Street]] and [[Inariyama Tunnel]]. == External Links == * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ Official Website of Ōishi Shrine] [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|Main Hall|left]] {{Clear}} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * **Black Thread Armor** and [[Human Mukotsu]] replica - owned by [[Mori Nagayoshi]] ([https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 Human Mukotsu replica forged by Akō swordsmith Sasaki Noriyuki during the late Edo period]). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|Ōishi Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Akō City buildings]] [[Category:History of Akō City]] [[Category:Akō incident]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Oishi Shrine]] [[Category:forcing refresh for Confirmed wikipedia 11s 6]] [[Category: of the shrine buildings in [[1935]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture Former Fusha]] [[Category:Akō Incident]] [[Category:Ōishi Yoshio]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]] }} [[sn:Oishi Shrine]] [[ja:大石神社]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Oishi Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|大石神社|version=104543748|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Haiden | location=[[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Akō City]], Upper Kariya, Former Castle Grounds | coordinates={{Coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}} | deity=Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio and the 47 loyal retainers, [[Kayano Shigemitsu|Kayano Sanpei]], Three Generations of the Asano Clan, Seven Generals of the Mori Clan | Rank=Former [[Prefectural Shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | Established=[[1900]] (Meiji 33) | Pilgrimage Route=[[Sacred Sites of Kami and Buddhas Pilgrimage]] No. 76 (Hyōgo No. 11) | Festival=[[December 14]] ([[Akō Gishi Festival]]) | Map={{location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=57|lon_deg=134|lon_min=23|lon_sec=19|label=Akō Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}}}} }} {{Commonscat|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} Located in [[Akō Castle]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Akō City]], the shrine is a merger of Akō Shrine and Ōishi Shrine. Its former [[modern shrine Rank system|Rank]] was prefectural, and it is now listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The principal deities enshrined are the ancestral spirits of the [[Asano clan]] and [[Mori clan]], former [[Akō Domain]] lords, as well as [[Ōishi Yoshio]] and the 47 loyal retainers, including [[Kayano Shigemitsu]], who committed seppuku midway through the raid. As the shrine is associated with fulfilling the great wish of avenging a lord, it is believed to grant blessings for "great wish fulfillment." === History === After the Akō incident, followers of the Akō Rōnin secretly enshrined them in a small shrine within the former Ōishi residence in Akō Castle. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the castle was sold, and the buildings were demolished, leaving the site desolate due to floods from the [[Chikusa River]]. In [[1900]] (Meiji 33), the government authorized the establishment of "Ōishi Shrine," but fundraising efforts for construction faced difficulties.Katayama Hakusen, "Senkai Oshō Nikki-shō" (Kagakuji, 1967) Opposition also came from [[right-wing]] and [[nationalist]] figures such as {{ill|Ōmachi Keigetsu|ja|大町桂月|ca|Ōmachi Keigetsu|12=draft}}.Seikyosha, "Nihon oyobi Nihonjin" (1909) Construction finally began in April [[1910]] (Meiji 43), and the shrine was completed in [[1912]] (Taishō 1). In [[1928]] (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to prefectural shrine status. After [[World War II]], the shrine merged with other shrines within and outside the castle grounds, including those dedicated to the Asano and Mori clans, as well as Inari Shrine and Ebisu Shrine, and was newly established as '''Akō Ōishi Shrine''' in [[2000]] (Heisei 12). === Korean Dogs === * The grounds feature statues of [[Komainu]] (Korean dogs), which are closer in appearance to dogs than the lion-like [[komainu]] typically found at other shrines. * The shrine houses historical artifacts such as a "Dog Receipt Certificate,"Wakisaka family documents: "Record of Dogs in the Castle Grounds" illustrating the historical connection between the Asano family and dogs in the Akō Domain. This shrine is one of the few in Japan that allows visitors to bring dogs. === Grounds === The shrine complex includes the main shrine, the worship hall, and several subordinate shrines. Facilities such as the Gishi Treasure Hall display artifacts related to the Akō Rōnin, while the Gishi Statue Hall exhibits wooden statues of the 47 retainers. * Main Hall – Built in [[1912]] (Taishō 1). * Worship Hall – Built in 1912. * Shrine Office * Subordinate Shrine Hall – Includes shrines such as [[Inari Shrine]], [[Awashima Shrine|Awashima Shrine]], and [[Tenmangū]]. * Gishi Historical Museum – A member of the [[Japan Museums Association]].[https://www.j-muse.or.jp/introduction/detail/?mid=2942 Akō Ōishi Shrine Gishi Historical Museum] Japan Museums Association Features a collection of relics related to the Akō Rōnin. * Gishi Statue Hall – Displays 49 sculptures of the loyal retainers by contemporary sculptors. === Cultural Properties === ==== National Historic Site ==== * Former Residence of Ōishi Yoshio – Managed by Akō City. === Festivals and Annual Events === * January 15 – [[Tondo]] Festival * April, 2nd Sunday – Spring Gishi Festival * May 5 – Ōishi Mochi Festival * June 30 – Rice Planting Festival * October, Sunday – Rice Harvesting Festival * December 14 – [[Akō Gishi Festival]] === Access === * 15-minute walk from [[JR West]]'s [[Akō Line]] [[Banshū-Akō Station]]. * 10-minute drive from [[Sanyō Expressway]] [[Akō Interchange]].Official shrine website, "History" section. === External Links === * [http://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/ Akō Ōishi Shrine] (official website) {{citation needed|Oishi Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine haiden.jpeg|270px]]
Worship Hall | location = 116 Nishinoyama Sakuranobabachō, Yamashina-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|58|12|N|135|47|44|E|}} | Deities = [[Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] | consecration_year = [[1935]] ([[Shōwa]] 10) | type = Former [[Fusha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | Main Festival = [[April 14]] (Spring Grand Festival), [[December 14]] (Gishi Festival) | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=12|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=44|label=Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamashina-ku]], [[Kyoto City]]. It enshrines [[Ōishi Yoshio]], also known as Ōishi Kuranosuke, the leader of the [[Akō Rōshi]] (47 Ronin). The shrine is situated near the location where Ōishi Yoshio lived in seclusion from July of [[Genroku]] 14 (1701) to September of Genroku 15 (1702) while planning the [[revenge]] of their lord. The shrine attracts worshippers who seek blessings for "great wish fulfillment" (大願成就), inspired by the determination of its enshrined deity. == History == In the early [[Shōwa]] era, a rōkyoku storyteller, Yoshida Yamato-no-Jō (second-generation [[Yoshida Narimaru]]), who deeply revered the Akō Rōshi, initiated the construction of a shrine dedicated to Ōishi Yoshio. With the support of local and governmental organizations, including the Governor of Kyoto serving as chair of the shrine construction committee, fundraising efforts led to the Completion tier 2]] (Shōwa 10). The shrine received permission for its establishment on [[December 9, 1933]] (Shōwa 8) and was designated as a [[Fusha]] in [[1937]] (Shōwa 12). == Features == * **Main Hall** - Built in [[1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Worship Hall** - Built in [[1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Gijinsya (Loyalty Shrine)** - A subordinate shrine enshrining [[Amano-ya Rihei]], who is said to have provided weapons for the assault on [[Kira Yoshinaka]]. * **Treasure Hall** - Houses artifacts and memorabilia associated with the Akō Rōshi. * **Administration Office** - Handles shrine operations. === Miniature Horse === The shrine keeps a miniature horse named "Hanako," which has been cared for within the precincts since [[2007]] (Heisei 19). == Festivals and Events == * **April 14** - Spring Grand Festival * **December 14** - Gishi Festival, commemorating the Akō Rōshi's raid. == Access == * 1-minute walk from the Ōishi Shrine-mae [[Keihan Bus]] stop. * 20 minutes by car from the [[Kyoto Higashi Interchange]] on the [[Meishin Expressway]]. * Close to [[Shinjūjō Street]] and [[Inariyama Tunnel]]. == External Links == * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ Official Website of Ōishi Shrine] [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|Main Hall|left]] {{Clear}} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * **Black Thread Armor** and [[Human Mukotsu]] replica - owned by [[Mori Nagayoshi]] ([https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 Human Mukotsu replica forged by Akō swordsmith Sasaki Noriyuki during the late Edo period]). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|Ōishi Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|大石神社|version=104543748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Akō City buildings]] [[Category:History of Akō City]] [[Category:Akō incident]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Oishi Shrine]] [[Category:forcing refresh for Confirmed wikipedia 11s 6]] [[Category: of the shrine buildings in [[1935]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture Former Fusha]] [[Category:Akō Incident]] [[Category:Ōishi Yoshio]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]] }} [[sn:Oishi Shrine]] [[ja:大石神社]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Oishi Shrine]]

{{Otheruses}} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[shrine]] dedicated to [[Ōishi Yoshio]] and the [[Akō Rōnin]] (Akō Gishi), who carried out the raid in the [[Akō incident]]. During the [[Edo period]], open veneration was impossible due to restrictions by the [[Edo shogunate]]. However, in [[1868]] (the first year of the [[Meiji]] era), [[Emperor Meiji]] sent an imperial envoy to [[Sengaku-ji]], where the Akō Rōnin are buried, to pay tribute. This inspired movements to establish a shrine within Akō Castle. In the late [[Taishō]] and early [[Shōwa]] periods, shrines dedicated to the Akō Rōnin were established in [[Akō City]] and [[Kyōto City]]. It is one of the shrines that became popular in the late Edo period for venerating [[feudal lords]]. == Akō Ōishi Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Akō Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan02s3.jpg|250px]]

Spring Grand Festival * **December 14** - Gishi Festival, commemorating the Akō Rōshi's raid. == Access == * 1-minute walk from the Ōishi Shrine-mae [[Keihan Bus]] stop. * 20 minutes by car from the [[Kyoto Higashi Interchange]] on the [[Meishin Expressway]]. * Close to [[Shinjūjō Street]] and [[Inariyama Tunnel]]. == External Links == * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ Official Website of Ōishi Shrine] [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|Main Hall|left]] {{Clear}} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * **Black Thread Armor** and [[Human Mukotsu]] replica - owned by [[Mori Nagayoshi]] ([https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 Human Mukotsu replica forged by Akō swordsmith Sasaki Noriyuki during the late Edo period]). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|Ōishi Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大石神社|version=104543748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Akō City buildings]] [[Category:History of Akō City]] [[Category:Akō incident]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Oishi Shrine]] [[Category:forcing refresh for Confirmed wikipedia 11s 6]] [[Category: of the shrine buildings in [[1935]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture Former Fusha]] [[Category:Akō Incident]] [[Category:Ōishi Yoshio]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]] }} [[sn:Oishi Shrine]] [[ja:大石神社]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Oishi Shrine]]

{{Otheruses}} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[shrine]] dedicated to [[Ōishi Yoshio]] and the [[Akō Rōnin]] (Akō Gishi), who carried out the raid in the [[Akō incident]]. During the [[Edo period]], open veneration was impossible due to restrictions by the [[Edo shogunate]]. However, in [[1868]] (the first year of the [[Meiji]] era), [[Emperor Meiji]] sent an imperial envoy to [[Sengaku-ji]], where the Akō Rōnin are buried, to pay tribute. This inspired movements to establish a shrine within Akō Castle. In the late [[Taishō]] and early [[Shōwa]] periods, shrines dedicated to the Akō Rōnin were established in [[Akō City]] and [[Kyōto City]]. It is one of the shrines that became popular in the late Edo period for venerating [[feudal lords]]. == Akō Ōishi Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Akō Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyogo pref Japan02s3.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location=[[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Akō City]], Upper Kariya, Former Castle Grounds | coordinates={{Coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}} | deity=Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio and the 47 loyal retainers, [[Kayano Shigemitsu|Kayano Sanpei]], Three Generations of the Asano Clan, Seven Generals of the Mori Clan | Rank=Former [[Prefectural Shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | Established=[[1900]] (Meiji 33) | Pilgrimage Route=[[Sacred Sites of Kami and Buddhas Pilgrimage]] No. 76 (Hyōgo No. 11) | Festival=[[December 14]] ([[Akō Gishi Festival]]) | Map={{location map|Japan Hyogo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=57|lon_deg=134|lon_min=23|lon_sec=19|label=Akō Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}}}} }} {{Commonscat|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} Located in [[Akō Castle]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Akō City]], the shrine is a merger of Akō Shrine and Ōishi Shrine. Its former [[modern shrine Rank system|Rank]] was prefectural, and it is now listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The principal deities enshrined are the ancestral spirits of the [[Asano clan]] and [[Mori clan]], former [[Akō Domain]] lords, as well as [[Ōishi Yoshio]] and the 47 loyal retainers, including [[Kayano Shigemitsu]], who committed seppuku midway through the raid. As the shrine is associated with fulfilling the great wish of avenging a lord, it is believed to grant blessings for "great wish fulfillment." === History === After the Akō incident, followers of the Akō Rōnin secretly enshrined them in a small shrine within the former Ōishi residence in Akō Castle. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the castle was sold, and the buildings were demolished, leaving the site desolate due to floods from the [[Chikusa River]]. In [[1900]] (Meiji 33), the government authorized the establishment of "Ōishi Shrine," but fundraising efforts for construction faced difficulties.Katayama Hakusen, "Senkai Oshō Nikki-shō" (Kagakuji, 1967) Opposition also came from [[right-wing]] and [[nationalist]] figures such as {{ill|Ōmachi Keigetsu|ja|大町桂月|ca|Ōmachi Keigetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Seikyosha, "Nihon oyobi Nihonjin" (1909) Construction finally began in April [[1910]] (Meiji 43), and the shrine was completed in [[1912]] (Taishō 1). In [[1928]] (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to prefectural shrine status. After [[World War II]], the shrine merged with other shrines within and outside the castle grounds, including those dedicated to the Asano and Mori clans, as well as Inari Shrine and Ebisu Shrine, and was newly established as '''Akō Ōishi Shrine''' in [[2000]] (Heisei 12). === Korean Dogs === * The grounds feature statues of [[Komainu]] (Korean dogs), which are closer in appearance to dogs than the lion-like [[komainu]] typically found at other shrines. * The shrine houses historical artifacts such as a "Dog Receipt Certificate,"Wakisaka family documents: "Record of Dogs in the Castle Grounds" illustrating the historical connection between the Asano family and dogs in the Akō Domain. This shrine is one of the few in Japan that allows visitors to bring dogs. === Grounds === The shrine complex includes the main shrine, the worship hall, and several subordinate shrines. Facilities such as the Gishi Treasure Hall display artifacts related to the Akō Rōnin, while the Gishi Statue Hall exhibits wooden statues of the 47 retainers. * Main Hall – Built in [[1912]] (Taishō 1). * Worship Hall – Built in 1912. * Shrine Office * Subordinate Shrine Hall – Includes shrines such as [[Inari Shrine]], [[Awashima Shrine|Awashima Shrine]], and [[Tenmangū]]. * Gishi Historical Museum – A member of the [[Japan Museums Association]].[https://www.j-muse.or.jp/introduction/detail/?mid=2942 Akō Ōishi Shrine Gishi Historical Museum] Japan Museums Association Features a collection of relics related to the Akō Rōnin. * Gishi Statue Hall – Displays 49 sculptures of the loyal retainers by contemporary sculptors. === Cultural Properties === ==== National Historic Site ==== * Former Residence of Ōishi Yoshio – Managed by Akō City. === Festivals and Annual Events === * January 15 – [[Tondo]] Festival * April, 2nd Sunday – Spring Gishi Festival * May 5 – Ōishi Mochi Festival * June 30 – Rice Planting Festival * October, Sunday – Rice Harvesting Festival * December 14 – [[Akō Gishi Festival]] === Access === * 15-minute walk from [[JR West]]'s [[Akō Line]] [[Banshū-Akō Station]]. * 10-minute drive from [[Sanyō Expressway]] [[Akō Interchange]].Official shrine website, "History" section. === External Links === * [http://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/ Akō Ōishi Shrine] (official website) {{citation needed|Oishi Shrine}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine haiden.jpeg|270px]]
Worship Hall | location = 116 Nishinoyama Sakuranobabachō, Yamashina-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|58|12|N|135|47|44|E|}} | Deities = [[Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] | consecration_year = [[1935]] ([[Shōwa]] 10) | type = Former [[Fusha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | Main Festival = [[April 14]] (Spring Grand Festival), [[December 14]] (Gishi Festival) | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=12|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=44|label=Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamashina-ku]], [[Kyoto City]]. It enshrines [[Ōishi Yoshio]], also known as Ōishi Kuranosuke, the leader of the [[Akō Rōshi]] (47 Ronin). The shrine is situated near the location where Ōishi Yoshio lived in seclusion from July of [[Genroku]] 14 (1701) to September of Genroku 15 (1702) while planning the [[revenge]] of their lord. The shrine attracts worshippers who seek blessings for "great wish fulfillment" (大願成就), inspired by the determination of its enshrined deity. == History == In the early [[Shōwa]] era, a rōkyoku storyteller, Yoshida Yamato-no-Jō (second-generation [[Yoshida Narimaru]]), who deeply revered the Akō Rōshi, initiated the construction of a shrine dedicated to Ōishi Yoshio. With the support of local and governmental organizations, including the Governor of Kyoto serving as chair of the shrine construction committee, fundraising efforts led to the Completion tier 2]] (Shōwa 10). The shrine received permission for its establishment on [[December 9, 1933]] (Shōwa 8) and was designated as a [[Fusha]] in [[1937]] (Shōwa 12). == Features == * **Main Hall** - Built in [[1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Worship Hall** - Built in [[1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Gijinsya (Loyalty Shrine)** - A subordinate shrine enshrining [[Amano-ya Rihei]], who is said to have provided weapons for the assault on [[Kira Yoshinaka]]. * **Treasure Hall** - Houses artifacts and memorabilia associated with the Akō Rōshi. * **Administration Office** - Handles shrine operations. === Miniature Horse === The shrine keeps a miniature horse named "Hanako," which has been cared for within the precincts since [[2007]] (Heisei 19). == Festivals and Events == * **April 14** - Spring Grand Festival * **December 14** - Gishi Festival, commemorating the Akō Rōshi's raid. == Access == * 1-minute walk from the Ōishi Shrine-mae [[Keihan Bus]] stop. * 20 minutes by car from the [[Kyoto Higashi Interchange]] on the [[Meishin Expressway]]. * Close to [[Shinjūjō Street]] and [[Inariyama Tunnel]]. == External Links == * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ Official Website of Ōishi Shrine] [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|Main Hall|left]] {{Clear}} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * **Black Thread Armor** and [[Human Mukotsu]] replica - owned by [[Mori Nagayoshi]] ([https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 Human Mukotsu replica forged by Akō swordsmith Sasaki Noriyuki during the late Edo period]). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} {{citation needed|Ōishi Shrine}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大石神社|version=104543748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Akō City buildings]] [[Category:History of Akō City]] [[Category:Akō incident]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Oishi Shrine]] [[Category:forcing refresh for Confirmed wikipedia 11s 6]] [[Category: of the shrine buildings in [[1935]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Kyoto Prefecture Former Fusha]] [[Category:Akō Incident]] [[Category:Ōishi Yoshio]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed category]] }} [[sn:Oishi Shrine]] [[ja:大石神社]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Oishi Shrine]]
