In the late [[Taishō]] and early [[Shōwa]] periods, shrines dedicated to the Akō Rōnin were established in [[Akō City]] and [[Kyōto City]]. It is one of the shrines that became popular in the late Edo period for venerating [[:en:Feudal lords|Feudal lords]]. == Akō Ōishi Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Akō Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyōgo pref Japan02s3.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location=[[Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Akō City]], Upper Kariya, Former Castle Grounds | coordinates={{Coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}} | deity=Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio and the 47 loyal retainers, [[Kayano Shigemitsu|Kayano Sanpei]], Three Generations of the Asano Clan, Seven Generals of the Mori Clan | Rank=Former [[Prefectural Shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | Established=[[:en:1900|1900]] (Meiji 33) | Pilgrimage Route=[[Sacred Sites of Kami and Buddhas Pilgrimage]] No. 76 (Hyōgo No. 11) | Festival=[[December 14]] ([[Akō Gishi Festival]]) | Map={{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=57|lon_deg=134|lon_min=23|lon_sec=19|label=Akō Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}}}} }} {{Commonscat|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} Located in [[:en:Akō Castle|Akō Castle]] in [[Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Akō City]], the shrine is a merger of Akō Shrine and Ōishi Shrine. Its former [[modern shrine Rank system|Rank]] was prefectural, and it is now listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The principal deities enshrined are the ancestral spirits of the [[:en:Asano clan|Asano clan]] and [[Mori clan]], former [[:en:Akō Domain|Akō Domain]] lords, as well as [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Yoshio]] and the 47 loyal retainers, including [[Kayano Shigemitsu]], who committed seppuku midway through the raid. As the shrine is associated with fulfilling the great wish of avenging a lord, it is believed to grant blessings for "great wish fulfillment." === History === After the Akō incident, followers of the Akō Rōnin secretly enshrined them in a small shrine within the former Ōishi residence in Akō Castle. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the castle was sold, and the buildings were demolished, leaving the site desolate due to floods from the [[Chikusa River]]. In [[:en:1900|1900]] (Meiji 33), the government authorized the establishment of "Ōishi Shrine," but fundraising efforts for construction faced difficulties.Katayama Hakusen, "Senkai Oshō Nikki-shō" (Kagakuji, 1967) Opposition also came from [[right-wing]] and [[nationalist]] figures such as {{ill|Ōmachi Keigetsu|ja|大町桂月|ca|Ōmachi Keigetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmachi Keigetsu|qq=}}.Seikyosha, "Nihon oyobi Nihonjin" (1909) Construction finally began in April [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43), and the shrine was completed in [[:en:1912|1912]] (Taishō 1). In [[:en:1928|1928]] (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to prefectural shrine status. After [[World War II]], the shrine merged with other shrines within and outside the castle grounds, including those dedicated to the Asano and Mori clans, as well as Inari Shrine and Ebisu Shrine, and was newly established as '''Akō Ōishi Shrine''' in [[:en:2000|2000]] (Heisei 12). === Korean Dogs === * The grounds feature statues of [[Komainu]] (Korean dogs), which are closer in appearance to dogs than the lion-like [[komainu]] typically found at other shrines. * The shrine houses historical artifacts such as a "Dog Receipt Certificate,"Wakisaka family documents: "Record of Dogs in the Castle Grounds" illustrating the historical connection between the Asano family and dogs in the Akō Domain. This shrine is one of the few in Japan that allows visitors to bring dogs. === Grounds === The shrine complex includes the main shrine, the worship hall, and several subordinate shrines. Facilities such as the Gishi Treasure Hall display artifacts related to the Akō Rōnin, while the Gishi Statue Hall exhibits wooden statues of the 47 retainers. * Main Hall – Built in [[:en:1912|1912]] (Taishō 1). * Worship Hall – Built in 1912. * Shrine Office * Subordinate Shrine Hall – Includes shrines such as [[Inari Shrine]], [[Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|Awashima Shrine]], and [[Tenmangū]]. * Gishi Historical Museum – A member of the [[Japan Museums Association]].[https://www.j-muse.or.jp/introduction/detail/?mid=2942 Akō Ōishi Shrine Gishi Historical Museum] Japan Museums Association Features a collection of relics related to the Akō Rōnin. * Gishi Statue Hall – Displays 49 sculptures of the loyal retainers by contemporary sculptors. === Cultural Properties === ==== National Historic Site ==== * Former Residence of Ōishi Yoshio – Managed by Akō City. === Festivals and Annual Events === * January 15 – [[Tondo]] Festival * April, 2nd Sunday – Spring Gishi Festival * May 5 – Ōishi Mochi Festival * June 30 – Rice Planting Festival * October, Sunday – Rice Harvesting Festival * December 14 – [[Akō Gishi Festival]] === Access === * 15-minute walk from [[JR West]]'s [[:en:Akō Line|Akō Line]] [[:en:Banshū-Akō Station|Banshū-Akō Station]]. * 10-minute drive from [[Sanyō Expressway]] [[Akō Interchange]].Official shrine website, "History" section. === External Links === * [http://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/ Akō Ōishi Shrine] (official website) {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine haiden.jpeg|270px]]
Worship Hall | location = 116 Nishinoyama Sakuranobabachō, Yamashina-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|58|12|N|135|47|44|E|}} | Deities = [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] | consecration_year = [[:en:1935|1935]] ([[Shōwa]] 10) | type = Former [[:en:Fusha|Fusha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | Main Festival = [[April 14]] (Spring Grand Festival), [[December 14]] (Gishi Festival) | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=12|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=44|label=Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamashina-ku]], [[Kyoto City]]. It enshrines [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Yoshio]], also known as Ōishi Kuranosuke, the leader of the [[:en:Akō Rōshi|Akō Rōshi]] (47 Ronin). The shrine is situated near the location where Ōishi Yoshio lived in seclusion from July of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 14 (1701) to September of Genroku 15 (1702) while planning the [[revenge]] of their lord. The shrine attracts worshippers who seek blessings for "great wish fulfillment" (大願成就), inspired by the determination of its enshrined deity. == History == In the early [[Shōwa]] era, a rōkyoku storyteller, Yoshida Yamato-no-Jō (second-generation [[Yoshida Narimaru]]), who deeply revered the Akō Rōshi, initiated the construction of a shrine dedicated to Ōishi Yoshio. With the support of local and governmental organizations, including the Governor of Kyoto serving as chair of the shrine construction committee, fundraising efforts led to the Completion tier 2]] (Shōwa 10). The shrine received permission for its establishment on [[December 9, 1933]] (Shōwa 8) and was designated as a [[:en:Fusha|Fusha]] in [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12). == Features == * **Main Hall** - Built in [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Worship Hall** - Built in [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Gijinsya (Loyalty Shrine)** - A subordinate shrine enshrining [[Amano-ya Rihei]], who is said to have provided weapons for the assault on [[Kira Yoshinaka]]. * **Treasure Hall** - Houses artifacts and memorabilia associated with the Akō Rōshi. * **Administration Office** - Handles shrine operations. === Miniature Horse === The shrine keeps a miniature horse named "Hanako," which has been cared for within the precincts since [[:en:2007|2007]] (Heisei 19). == Festivals and Events == * **April 14** - Spring Grand Festival * **December 14** - Gishi Festival, commemorating the Akō Rōshi's raid. == Access == * 1-minute walk from the Ōishi Shrine-mae [[Keihan Bus]] stop. * 20 minutes by car from the [[Kyoto Higashi Interchange]] on the [[Meishin Expressway]]. * Close to [[Shinjūjō Street]] and [[Inariyama Tunnel]]. == External Links == * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ Official Website of Ōishi Shrine] [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|Main Hall|left]] {{Clear}} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * **Black Thread Armor** and [[Human Mukotsu]] replica - owned by [[Mori Nagayoshi]] ([https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 Human Mukotsu replica forged by Akō swordsmith Sasaki Noriyuki during the late Edo period]). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大石神社|version=104543748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: of the shrine buildings in [[:en:1935|1935]] [[Category:Shrines in Kyoto Prefecture]] [[Category:Shrines in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] }} [[sn:Oishi Shrine]] [[ja:大石神社]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Oishi Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:National historic sites in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:赤穂事件]] [[Category:Buildings in Akō]] [[Category:History of Ako, Hyogo]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]]

Facilities such as the Gishi Treasure Hall display artifacts related to the Akō Rōnin, while the Gishi Statue Hall exhibits wooden statues of the 47 retainers. * Main Hall – Built in [[:en:1912|1912]] (Taishō 1). * Worship Hall – Built in 1912. * Shrine Office * Subordinate Shrine Hall – Includes shrines such as [[Inari Shrine]], [[Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|Awashima Shrine]], and [[Tenmangū]]. * Gishi Historical Museum – A member of the [[Japan Museums Association]].[https://www.j-muse.or.jp/introduction/detail/?mid=2942 Akō Ōishi Shrine Gishi Historical Museum] Japan Museums Association Features a collection of relics related to the Akō Rōnin. * Gishi Statue Hall – Displays 49 sculptures of the loyal retainers by contemporary sculptors. === Cultural Properties === ==== National Historic Site ==== * Former Residence of Ōishi Yoshio – Managed by Akō City. === Festivals and Annual Events === * January 15 – [[Tondo]] Festival * April, 2nd Sunday – Spring Gishi Festival * May 5 – Ōishi Mochi Festival * June 30 – Rice Planting Festival * October, Sunday – Rice Harvesting Festival * December 14 – [[Akō Gishi Festival]] === Access === * 15-minute walk from [[JR West]]'s [[:en:Akō Line|Akō Line]] [[:en:Banshū-Akō Station|Banshū-Akō Station]]. * 10-minute drive from [[Sanyō Expressway]] [[Akō Interchange]].Official shrine website, "History" section. === External Links === * [http://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/ Akō Ōishi Shrine] (official website) {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine haiden.jpeg|270px]]
Worship Hall | location = 116 Nishinoyama Sakuranobabachō, Yamashina-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|58|12|N|135|47|44|E|}} | Deities = [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] | consecration_year = [[:en:1935|1935]] ([[Shōwa]] 10) | type = Former [[:en:Fusha|Fusha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | Main Festival = [[April 14]] (Spring Grand Festival), [[December 14]] (Gishi Festival) | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=12|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=44|label=Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamashina-ku]], [[Kyoto City]]. It enshrines [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Yoshio]], also known as Ōishi Kuranosuke, the leader of the [[:en:Akō Rōshi|Akō Rōshi]] (47 Ronin). The shrine is situated near the location where Ōishi Yoshio lived in seclusion from July of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 14 (1701) to September of Genroku 15 (1702) while planning the [[revenge]] of their lord. The shrine attracts worshippers who seek blessings for "great wish fulfillment" (大願成就), inspired by the determination of its enshrined deity. == History == In the early [[Shōwa]] era, a rōkyoku storyteller, Yoshida Yamato-no-Jō (second-generation [[Yoshida Narimaru]]), who deeply revered the Akō Rōshi, initiated the construction of a shrine dedicated to Ōishi Yoshio. With the support of local and governmental organizations, including the Governor of Kyoto serving as chair of the shrine construction committee, fundraising efforts led to the Completion tier 2]] (Shōwa 10). The shrine received permission for its establishment on [[December 9, 1933]] (Shōwa 8) and was designated as a [[:en:Fusha|Fusha]] in [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12). == Features == * **Main Hall** - Built in [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Worship Hall** - Built in [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Gijinsya (Loyalty Shrine)** - A subordinate shrine enshrining [[Amano-ya Rihei]], who is said to have provided weapons for the assault on [[Kira Yoshinaka]]. * **Treasure Hall** - Houses artifacts and memorabilia associated with the Akō Rōshi. * **Administration Office** - Handles shrine operations. === Miniature Horse === The shrine keeps a miniature horse named "Hanako," which has been cared for within the precincts since [[:en:2007|2007]] (Heisei 19). == Festivals and Events == * **April 14** - Spring Grand Festival * **December 14** - Gishi Festival, commemorating the Akō Rōshi's raid. == Access == * 1-minute walk from the Ōishi Shrine-mae [[Keihan Bus]] stop. * 20 minutes by car from the [[Kyoto Higashi Interchange]] on the [[Meishin Expressway]]. * Close to [[Shinjūjō Street]] and [[Inariyama Tunnel]]. == External Links == * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ Official Website of Ōishi Shrine] [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|Main Hall|left]] {{Clear}} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * **Black Thread Armor** and [[Human Mukotsu]] replica - owned by [[Mori Nagayoshi]] ([https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 Human Mukotsu replica forged by Akō swordsmith Sasaki Noriyuki during the late Edo period]). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大石神社|version=104543748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: of the shrine buildings in [[:en:1935|1935]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] }} [[sn:Oishi Shrine]] [[ja:大石神社]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Oishi Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:National historic sites in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:赤穂事件]] [[Category:Buildings in Akō]] [[Category:History of Ako, Hyogo]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses}} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Yoshio]] and the [[Akō Rōnin]] (Akō Gishi), who carried out the raid in the [[:en:Akō incident|Akō incident]]. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], open veneration was impossible due to restrictions by the [[:en:Edo shogunate|Edo shogunate]]. However, in [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of the [[Meiji]] era), [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] sent an imperial envoy to [[Sengaku-ji]], where the Akō Rōnin are buried, to pay tribute. This inspired movements to establish a shrine within Akō Castle. In the late [[Taishō]] and early [[Shōwa]] periods, shrines dedicated to the Akō Rōnin were established in [[Akō City]] and [[Kyōto City]]. It is one of the shrines that became popular in the late Edo period for venerating [[:en:Feudal lords|Feudal lords]]. == Akō Ōishi Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Akō Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyōgo pref Japan02s3.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location=[[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Akō City]], Upper Kariya, Former Castle Grounds | coordinates={{Coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}} | deity=Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio and the 47 loyal retainers, [[Kayano Shigemitsu|Kayano Sanpei]], Three Generations of the Asano Clan, Seven Generals of the Mori Clan | Rank=Former [[Prefectural Shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | Established=[[:en:1900|1900]] (Meiji 33) | Pilgrimage Route=[[Sacred Sites of Kami and Buddhas Pilgrimage]] No. 76 (Hyōgo No. 11) | Festival=[[December 14]] ([[Akō Gishi Festival]]) | Map={{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=57|lon_deg=134|lon_min=23|lon_sec=19|label=Akō Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}}}} }} {{Commonscat|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} Located in [[:en:Akō Castle|Akō Castle]] in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Akō City]], the shrine is a merger of Akō Shrine and Ōishi Shrine. Its former [[modern shrine Rank system|Rank]] was prefectural, and it is now listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The principal deities enshrined are the ancestral spirits of the [[:en:Asano clan|Asano clan]] and [[Mori clan]], former [[:en:Akō Domain|Akō Domain]] lords, as well as [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Yoshio]] and the 47 loyal retainers, including [[Kayano Shigemitsu]], who committed seppuku midway through the raid. As the shrine is associated with fulfilling the great wish of avenging a lord, it is believed to grant blessings for "great wish fulfillment." === History === After the Akō incident, followers of the Akō Rōnin secretly enshrined them in a small shrine within the former Ōishi residence in Akō Castle. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the castle was sold, and the buildings were demolished, leaving the site desolate due to floods from the [[Chikusa River]]. In [[:en:1900|1900]] (Meiji 33), the government authorized the establishment of "Ōishi Shrine," but fundraising efforts for construction faced difficulties.Katayama Hakusen, "Senkai Oshō Nikki-shō" (Kagakuji, 1967) Opposition also came from [[right-wing]] and [[nationalist]] figures such as {{ill|Ōmachi Keigetsu|ja|大町桂月|ca|Ōmachi Keigetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmachi Keigetsu|qq=}}.Seikyosha, "Nihon oyobi Nihonjin" (1909) Construction finally began in April [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43), and the shrine was completed in [[:en:1912|1912]] (Taishō 1). In [[:en:1928|1928]] (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to prefectural shrine status. After [[World War II]], the shrine merged with other shrines within and outside the castle grounds, including those dedicated to the Asano and Mori clans, as well as Inari Shrine and Ebisu Shrine, and was newly established as '''Akō Ōishi Shrine''' in [[:en:2000|2000]] (Heisei 12). === Korean Dogs === * The grounds feature statues of [[Komainu]] (Korean dogs), which are closer in appearance to dogs than the lion-like [[komainu]] typically found at other shrines. * The shrine houses historical artifacts such as a "Dog Receipt Certificate,"Wakisaka family documents: "Record of Dogs in the Castle Grounds" illustrating the historical connection between the Asano family and dogs in the Akō Domain. This shrine is one of the few in Japan that allows visitors to bring dogs. === Grounds === The shrine complex includes the main shrine, the worship hall, and several subordinate shrines. Facilities such as the Gishi Treasure Hall display artifacts related to the Akō Rōnin, while the Gishi Statue Hall exhibits wooden statues of the 47 retainers. * Main Hall – Built in [[:en:1912|1912]] (Taishō 1). * Worship Hall – Built in 1912. * Shrine Office * Subordinate Shrine Hall – Includes shrines such as [[Inari Shrine]], [[Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|Awashima Shrine]], and [[Tenmangū]]. * Gishi Historical Museum – A member of the [[Japan Museums Association]].[https://www.j-muse.or.jp/introduction/detail/?mid=2942 Akō Ōishi Shrine Gishi Historical Museum] Japan Museums Association Features a collection of relics related to the Akō Rōnin. * Gishi Statue Hall – Displays 49 sculptures of the loyal retainers by contemporary sculptors. === Cultural Properties === ==== National Historic Site ==== * Former Residence of Ōishi Yoshio – Managed by Akō City. === Festivals and Annual Events === * January 15 – [[Tondo]] Festival * April, 2nd Sunday – Spring Gishi Festival * May 5 – Ōishi Mochi Festival * June 30 – Rice Planting Festival * October, Sunday – Rice Harvesting Festival * December 14 – [[Akō Gishi Festival]] === Access === * 15-minute walk from [[JR West]]'s [[:en:Akō Line|Akō Line]] [[:en:Banshū-Akō Station|Banshū-Akō Station]]. * 10-minute drive from [[Sanyō Expressway]] [[Akō Interchange]].Official shrine website, "History" section. === External Links === * [http://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/ Akō Ōishi Shrine] (official website) {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine haiden.jpeg|270px]]
Worship Hall | location = 116 Nishinoyama Sakuranobabachō, Yamashina-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|58|12|N|135|47|44|E|}} | Deities = [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] | consecration_year = [[:en:1935|1935]] ([[Shōwa]] 10) | type = Former [[:en:Fusha|Fusha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | Main Festival = [[April 14]] (Spring Grand Festival), [[December 14]] (Gishi Festival) | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=12|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=44|label=Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamashina-ku]], [[Kyoto City]]. It enshrines [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Yoshio]], also known as Ōishi Kuranosuke, the leader of the [[:en:Akō Rōshi|Akō Rōshi]] (47 Ronin). The shrine is situated near the location where Ōishi Yoshio lived in seclusion from July of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 14 (1701) to September of Genroku 15 (1702) while planning the [[revenge]] of their lord. The shrine attracts worshippers who seek blessings for "great wish fulfillment" (大願成就), inspired by the determination of its enshrined deity. == History == In the early [[Shōwa]] era, a rōkyoku storyteller, Yoshida Yamato-no-Jō (second-generation [[Yoshida Narimaru]]), who deeply revered the Akō Rōshi, initiated the construction of a shrine dedicated to Ōishi Yoshio. With the support of local and governmental organizations, including the Governor of Kyoto serving as chair of the shrine construction committee, fundraising efforts led to the Completion tier 2]] (Shōwa 10). The shrine received permission for its establishment on [[December 9, 1933]] (Shōwa 8) and was designated as a [[:en:Fusha|Fusha]] in [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12). == Features == * **Main Hall** - Built in [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Worship Hall** - Built in [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Gijinsya (Loyalty Shrine)** - A subordinate shrine enshrining [[Amano-ya Rihei]], who is said to have provided weapons for the assault on [[Kira Yoshinaka]]. * **Treasure Hall** - Houses artifacts and memorabilia associated with the Akō Rōshi. * **Administration Office** - Handles shrine operations. === Miniature Horse === The shrine keeps a miniature horse named "Hanako," which has been cared for within the precincts since [[:en:2007|2007]] (Heisei 19). == Festivals and Events == * **April 14** - Spring Grand Festival * **December 14** - Gishi Festival, commemorating the Akō Rōshi's raid. == Access == * 1-minute walk from the Ōishi Shrine-mae [[Keihan Bus]] stop. * 20 minutes by car from the [[Kyoto Higashi Interchange]] on the [[Meishin Expressway]]. * Close to [[Shinjūjō Street]] and [[Inariyama Tunnel]]. == External Links == * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ Official Website of Ōishi Shrine] [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|Main Hall|left]] {{Clear}} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * **Black Thread Armor** and [[Human Mukotsu]] replica - owned by [[Mori Nagayoshi]] ([https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 Human Mukotsu replica forged by Akō swordsmith Sasaki Noriyuki during the late Edo period]). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大石神社|version=104543748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: of the shrine buildings in [[:en:1935|1935]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] }} [[sn:Oishi Shrine]] [[ja:大石神社]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Oishi Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:National historic sites in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:赤穂事件]] [[Category:Buildings in Akō]] [[Category:History of Ako, Hyogo]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]]

Spring Grand Festival * **December 14** - Gishi Festival, commemorating the Akō Rōshi's raid. == Access == * 1-minute walk from the Ōishi Shrine-mae [[Keihan Bus]] stop. * 20 minutes by car from the [[Kyoto Higashi Interchange]] on the [[Meishin Expressway]]. * Close to [[Shinjūjō Street]] and [[Inariyama Tunnel]]. == External Links == * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ Official Website of Ōishi Shrine] [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|Main Hall|left]] {{Clear}} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * **Black Thread Armor** and [[Human Mukotsu]] replica - owned by [[Mori Nagayoshi]] ([https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 Human Mukotsu replica forged by Akō swordsmith Sasaki Noriyuki during the late Edo period]). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大石神社|version=104543748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: of the shrine buildings in [[:en:1935|1935]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] }} [[sn:Oishi Shrine]] [[ja:大石神社]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Oishi Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:National historic sites in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:赤穂事件]] [[Category:Buildings in Akō]] [[Category:History of Ako, Hyogo]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Otheruses}} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Yoshio]] and the [[Akō Rōnin]] (Akō Gishi), who carried out the raid in the [[:en:Akō incident|Akō incident]]. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], open veneration was impossible due to restrictions by the [[:en:Edo shogunate|Edo shogunate]]. However, in [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of the [[Meiji]] era), [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] sent an imperial envoy to [[Sengaku-ji]], where the Akō Rōnin are buried, to pay tribute. This inspired movements to establish a shrine within Akō Castle. In the late [[Taishō]] and early [[Shōwa]] periods, shrines dedicated to the Akō Rōnin were established in [[Akō City]] and [[Kyōto City]]. It is one of the shrines that became popular in the late Edo period for venerating [[:en:Feudal lords|Feudal lords]]. == Akō Ōishi Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Akō Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyōgo pref Japan02s3.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location=[[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Akō City]], Upper Kariya, Former Castle Grounds | coordinates={{Coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}} | deity=Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio and the 47 loyal retainers, [[Kayano Shigemitsu|Kayano Sanpei]], Three Generations of the Asano Clan, Seven Generals of the Mori Clan | Rank=Former [[Prefectural Shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | Established=[[:en:1900|1900]] (Meiji 33) | Pilgrimage Route=[[Sacred Sites of Kami and Buddhas Pilgrimage]] No. 76 (Hyōgo No. 11) | Festival=[[December 14]] ([[Akō Gishi Festival]]) | Map={{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=57|lon_deg=134|lon_min=23|lon_sec=19|label=Akō Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}}}} }} {{Commonscat|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} Located in [[:en:Akō Castle|Akō Castle]] in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Akō City]], the shrine is a merger of Akō Shrine and Ōishi Shrine. Its former [[modern shrine Rank system|Rank]] was prefectural, and it is now listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The principal deities enshrined are the ancestral spirits of the [[:en:Asano clan|Asano clan]] and [[Mori clan]], former [[:en:Akō Domain|Akō Domain]] lords, as well as [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Yoshio]] and the 47 loyal retainers, including [[Kayano Shigemitsu]], who committed seppuku midway through the raid. As the shrine is associated with fulfilling the great wish of avenging a lord, it is believed to grant blessings for "great wish fulfillment." === History === After the Akō incident, followers of the Akō Rōnin secretly enshrined them in a small shrine within the former Ōishi residence in Akō Castle. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the castle was sold, and the buildings were demolished, leaving the site desolate due to floods from the [[Chikusa River]]. In [[:en:1900|1900]] (Meiji 33), the government authorized the establishment of "Ōishi Shrine," but fundraising efforts for construction faced difficulties.Katayama Hakusen, "Senkai Oshō Nikki-shō" (Kagakuji, 1967) Opposition also came from [[right-wing]] and [[nationalist]] figures such as {{ill|Ōmachi Keigetsu|ja|大町桂月|ca|Ōmachi Keigetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmachi Keigetsu|qq=}}.Seikyosha, "Nihon oyobi Nihonjin" (1909) Construction finally began in April [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43), and the shrine was completed in [[:en:1912|1912]] (Taishō 1). In [[:en:1928|1928]] (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to prefectural shrine status. After [[World War II]], the shrine merged with other shrines within and outside the castle grounds, including those dedicated to the Asano and Mori clans, as well as Inari Shrine and Ebisu Shrine, and was newly established as '''Akō Ōishi Shrine''' in [[:en:2000|2000]] (Heisei 12). === Korean Dogs === * The grounds feature statues of [[Komainu]] (Korean dogs), which are closer in appearance to dogs than the lion-like [[komainu]] typically found at other shrines. * The shrine houses historical artifacts such as a "Dog Receipt Certificate,"Wakisaka family documents: "Record of Dogs in the Castle Grounds" illustrating the historical connection between the Asano family and dogs in the Akō Domain. This shrine is one of the few in Japan that allows visitors to bring dogs. === Grounds === The shrine complex includes the main shrine, the worship hall, and several subordinate shrines. Facilities such as the Gishi Treasure Hall display artifacts related to the Akō Rōnin, while the Gishi Statue Hall exhibits wooden statues of the 47 retainers. * Main Hall – Built in [[:en:1912|1912]] (Taishō 1). * Worship Hall – Built in 1912. * Shrine Office * Subordinate Shrine Hall – Includes shrines such as [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrine]], [[Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|Awashima Shrine]], and [[Tenmangū]]. * Gishi Historical Museum – A member of the [[Japan Museums Association]].[https://www.j-muse.or.jp/introduction/detail/?mid=2942 Akō Ōishi Shrine Gishi Historical Museum] Japan Museums Association Features a collection of relics related to the Akō Rōnin. * Gishi Statue Hall – Displays 49 sculptures of the loyal retainers by contemporary sculptors. === Cultural Properties === ==== National Historic Site ==== * Former Residence of Ōishi Yoshio – Managed by Akō City. === Festivals and Annual Events === * January 15 – [[Tondo]] Festival * April, 2nd Sunday – Spring Gishi Festival * May 5 – Ōishi Mochi Festival * June 30 – Rice Planting Festival * October, Sunday – Rice Harvesting Festival * December 14 – [[Akō Gishi Festival]] === Access === * 15-minute walk from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]'s [[:en:Akō Line|Akō Line]] [[:en:Banshū-Akō Station|Banshū-Akō Station]]. * 10-minute drive from [[Sanyō Expressway]] [[Akō Interchange]].Official shrine website, "History" section. === External Links === * [http://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/ Akō Ōishi Shrine] (official website) {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine haiden.jpeg|270px]]
Worship Hall | location = 116 Nishinoyama Sakuranobabachō, Yamashina-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|58|12|N|135|47|44|E|}} | Deities = [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] | consecration_year = [[:en:1935|1935]] ([[Shōwa]] 10) | type = Former [[:en:Fusha|Fusha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | Main Festival = [[April 14]] (Spring Grand Festival), [[December 14]] (Gishi Festival) | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=12|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=44|label=Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamashina-ku]], [[Kyoto City]]. It enshrines [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Yoshio]], also known as Ōishi Kuranosuke, the leader of the [[:en:Akō Rōshi|Akō Rōshi]] (47 Ronin). The shrine is situated near the location where Ōishi Yoshio lived in seclusion from July of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 14 (1701) to September of Genroku 15 (1702) while planning the [[revenge]] of their lord. The shrine attracts worshippers who seek blessings for "great wish fulfillment" (大願成就), inspired by the determination of its enshrined deity. == History == In the early [[Shōwa]] era, a rōkyoku storyteller, Yoshida Yamato-no-Jō (second-generation [[Yoshida Narimaru]]), who deeply revered the Akō Rōshi, initiated the construction of a shrine dedicated to Ōishi Yoshio. With the support of local and governmental organizations, including the Governor of Kyoto serving as chair of the shrine construction committee, fundraising efforts led to the Completion tier 2]] (Shōwa 10). The shrine received permission for its establishment on [[December 9, 1933]] (Shōwa 8) and was designated as a [[:en:Fusha|Fusha]] in [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12). == Features == * **Main Hall** - Built in [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Worship Hall** - Built in [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Gijinsya (Loyalty Shrine)** - A subordinate shrine enshrining [[Amano-ya Rihei]], who is said to have provided weapons for the assault on [[Kira Yoshinaka]]. * **Treasure Hall** - Houses artifacts and memorabilia associated with the Akō Rōshi. * **Administration Office** - Handles shrine operations. === Miniature Horse === The shrine keeps a miniature horse named "Hanako," which has been cared for within the precincts since [[:en:2007|2007]] (Heisei 19). == Festivals and Events == * **April 14** - Spring Grand Festival * **December 14** - Gishi Festival, commemorating the Akō Rōshi's raid. == Access == * 1-minute walk from the Ōishi Shrine-mae [[Keihan Bus]] stop. * 20 minutes by car from the [[Kyoto Higashi Interchange]] on the [[Meishin Expressway]]. * Close to [[Shinjūjō Street]] and [[Inariyama Tunnel]]. == External Links == * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ Official Website of Ōishi Shrine] [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|Main Hall|left]] {{Clear}} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * **Black Thread Armor** and [[Human Mukotsu]] replica - owned by [[Mori Nagayoshi]] ([https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 Human Mukotsu replica forged by Akō swordsmith Sasaki Noriyuki during the late Edo period]). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大石神社|version=104543748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: of the shrine buildings in [[:en:1935|1935]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] }} [[sn:Oishi Shrine]] [[ja:大石神社]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Oishi Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:National historic sites in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:赤穂事件]] [[Category:Buildings in Akō]] [[Category:History of Ako, Hyogo]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]]

In the late [[Taishō]] and early [[Shōwa]] periods, shrines dedicated to the Akō Rōnin were established in [[Akō City]] and [[Kyōto City]]. It is one of the shrines that became popular in the late Edo period for venerating [[:en:Feudal lords|Feudal lords]]. == Akō Ōishi Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Akō Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyōgo pref Japan02s3.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location=[[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Akō City]], Upper Kariya, Former Castle Grounds | coordinates={{Coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}} | deity=Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio and the 47 loyal retainers, [[Kayano Shigemitsu|Kayano Sanpei]], Three Generations of the Asano Clan, Seven Generals of the Mori Clan | Rank=Former [[Prefectural Shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | Established=[[:en:1900|1900]] (Meiji 33) | Pilgrimage Route=[[Sacred Sites of Kami and Buddhas Pilgrimage]] No. 76 (Hyōgo No. 11) | Festival=[[December 14]] ([[Akō Gishi Festival]]) | Map={{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=57|lon_deg=134|lon_min=23|lon_sec=19|label=Akō Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}}}} }} {{Commonscat|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} Located in [[:en:Akō Castle|Akō Castle]] in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Akō City]], the shrine is a merger of Akō Shrine and Ōishi Shrine. Its former [[modern shrine Rank system|Rank]] was prefectural, and it is now listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The principal deities enshrined are the ancestral spirits of the [[:en:Asano clan|Asano clan]] and [[Mori clan]], former [[:en:Akō Domain|Akō Domain]] lords, as well as [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Yoshio]] and the 47 loyal retainers, including [[Kayano Shigemitsu]], who committed seppuku midway through the raid. As the shrine is associated with fulfilling the great wish of avenging a lord, it is believed to grant blessings for "great wish fulfillment." === History === After the Akō incident, followers of the Akō Rōnin secretly enshrined them in a small shrine within the former Ōishi residence in Akō Castle. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the castle was sold, and the buildings were demolished, leaving the site desolate due to floods from the [[Chikusa River]]. In [[:en:1900|1900]] (Meiji 33), the government authorized the establishment of "Ōishi Shrine," but fundraising efforts for construction faced difficulties.Katayama Hakusen, "Senkai Oshō Nikki-shō" (Kagakuji, 1967) Opposition also came from [[right-wing]] and [[nationalist]] figures such as {{ill|Ōmachi Keigetsu|ja|大町桂月|ca|Ōmachi Keigetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmachi Keigetsu|qq=}}.Seikyosha, "Nihon oyobi Nihonjin" (1909) Construction finally began in April [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43), and the shrine was completed in [[:en:1912|1912]] (Taishō 1). In [[:en:1928|1928]] (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to prefectural shrine status. After [[World War II]], the shrine merged with other shrines within and outside the castle grounds, including those dedicated to the Asano and Mori clans, as well as Inari Shrine and Ebisu Shrine, and was newly established as '''Akō Ōishi Shrine''' in [[:en:2000|2000]] (Heisei 12). === Korean Dogs === * The grounds feature statues of [[Komainu]] (Korean dogs), which are closer in appearance to dogs than the lion-like [[komainu]] typically found at other shrines. * The shrine houses historical artifacts such as a "Dog Receipt Certificate,"Wakisaka family documents: "Record of Dogs in the Castle Grounds" illustrating the historical connection between the Asano family and dogs in the Akō Domain. This shrine is one of the few in Japan that allows visitors to bring dogs. === Grounds === The shrine complex includes the main shrine, the worship hall, and several subordinate shrines. Facilities such as the Gishi Treasure Hall display artifacts related to the Akō Rōnin, while the Gishi Statue Hall exhibits wooden statues of the 47 retainers. * Main Hall – Built in [[:en:1912|1912]] (Taishō 1). * Worship Hall – Built in 1912. * Shrine Office * Subordinate Shrine Hall – Includes shrines such as [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrine]], [[Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|Awashima Shrine]], and [[Tenmangū]]. * Gishi Historical Museum – A member of the [[Japan Museums Association]].[https://www.j-muse.or.jp/introduction/detail/?mid=2942 Akō Ōishi Shrine Gishi Historical Museum] Japan Museums Association Features a collection of relics related to the Akō Rōnin. * Gishi Statue Hall – Displays 49 sculptures of the loyal retainers by contemporary sculptors. === Cultural Properties === ==== National Historic Site ==== * Former Residence of Ōishi Yoshio – Managed by Akō City. === Festivals and Annual Events === * January 15 – [[Tondo]] Festival * April, 2nd Sunday – Spring Gishi Festival * May 5 – Ōishi Mochi Festival * June 30 – Rice Planting Festival * October, Sunday – Rice Harvesting Festival * December 14 – [[Akō Gishi Festival]] === Access === * 15-minute walk from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]'s [[:en:Akō Line|Akō Line]] [[:en:Banshū-Akō Station|Banshū-Akō Station]]. * 10-minute drive from [[Sanyō Expressway]] [[Akō Interchange]].Official shrine website, "History" section. === External Links === * [http://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/ Akō Ōishi Shrine] (official website) {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine haiden.jpeg|270px]]
Worship Hall | location = 116 Nishinoyama Sakuranobabachō, Yamashina-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|58|12|N|135|47|44|E|}} | Deities = [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] | consecration_year = [[:en:1935|1935]] ([[Shōwa]] 10) | type = Former [[:en:Fusha|Fusha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | Main Festival = [[April 14]] (Spring Grand Festival), [[December 14]] (Gishi Festival) | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=12|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=44|label=Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamashina-ku]], [[Kyoto City]]. It enshrines [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Yoshio]], also known as Ōishi Kuranosuke, the leader of the [[:en:Akō Rōshi|Akō Rōshi]] (47 Ronin). The shrine is situated near the location where Ōishi Yoshio lived in seclusion from July of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 14 (1701) to September of Genroku 15 (1702) while planning the [[revenge]] of their lord. The shrine attracts worshippers who seek blessings for "great wish fulfillment" (大願成就), inspired by the determination of its enshrined deity. == History == In the early [[Shōwa]] era, a rōkyoku storyteller, Yoshida Yamato-no-Jō (second-generation [[Yoshida Narimaru]]), who deeply revered the Akō Rōshi, initiated the construction of a shrine dedicated to Ōishi Yoshio. With the support of local and governmental organizations, including the Governor of Kyoto serving as chair of the shrine construction committee, fundraising efforts led to the Completion tier 2]] (Shōwa 10). The shrine received permission for its establishment on [[December 9, 1933]] (Shōwa 8) and was designated as a [[:en:Fusha|Fusha]] in [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12). == Features == * **Main Hall** - Built in [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Worship Hall** - Built in [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Gijinsya (Loyalty Shrine)** - A subordinate shrine enshrining [[Amano-ya Rihei]], who is said to have provided weapons for the assault on [[:en:Kira Yoshinaka|Kira Yoshinaka]]. * **Treasure Hall** - Houses artifacts and memorabilia associated with the Akō Rōshi. * **Administration Office** - Handles shrine operations. === Miniature Horse === The shrine keeps a miniature horse named "Hanako," which has been cared for within the precincts since [[:en:2007|2007]] (Heisei 19). == Festivals and Events == * **April 14** - Spring Grand Festival * **December 14** - Gishi Festival, commemorating the Akō Rōshi's raid. == Access == * 1-minute walk from the Ōishi Shrine-mae [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]] stop. * 20 minutes by car from the [[Kyoto Higashi Interchange]] on the [[Meishin Expressway]]. * Close to [[Shinjūjō Street]] and [[Inariyama Tunnel]]. == External Links == * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ Official Website of Ōishi Shrine] [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|Main Hall|left]] {{Clear}} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * **Black Thread Armor** and [[Human Mukotsu]] replica - owned by [[Mori Nagayoshi]] ([https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 Human Mukotsu replica forged by Akō swordsmith Sasaki Noriyuki during the late Edo period]). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大石神社|version=104543748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: of the shrine buildings in [[:en:1935|1935]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] }} [[sn:Oishi Shrine]] [[ja:大石神社]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Oishi Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:National historic sites in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:赤穂事件]] [[Category:Buildings in Akō]] [[Category:History of Ako, Hyogo]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]]

Facilities such as the Gishi Treasure Hall display artifacts related to the Akō Rōnin, while the Gishi Statue Hall exhibits wooden statues of the 47 retainers. * Main Hall – Built in [[:en:1912|1912]] (Taishō 1). * Worship Hall – Built in 1912. * Shrine Office * Subordinate Shrine Hall – Includes shrines such as [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrine]], [[Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|Awashima Shrine]], and [[Tenmangū]]. * Gishi Historical Museum – A member of the [[Japan Museums Association]].[https://www.j-muse.or.jp/introduction/detail/?mid=2942 Akō Ōishi Shrine Gishi Historical Museum] Japan Museums Association Features a collection of relics related to the Akō Rōnin. * Gishi Statue Hall – Displays 49 sculptures of the loyal retainers by contemporary sculptors. === Cultural Properties === ==== National Historic Site ==== * Former Residence of Ōishi Yoshio – Managed by Akō City. === Festivals and Annual Events === * January 15 – [[Tondo]] Festival * April, 2nd Sunday – Spring Gishi Festival * May 5 – Ōishi Mochi Festival * June 30 – Rice Planting Festival * October, Sunday – Rice Harvesting Festival * December 14 – [[Akō Gishi Festival]] === Access === * 15-minute walk from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]'s [[:en:Akō Line|Akō Line]] [[:en:Banshū-Akō Station|Banshū-Akō Station]]. * 10-minute drive from [[Sanyō Expressway]] [[Akō Interchange]].Official shrine website, "History" section. === External Links === * [http://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/ Akō Ōishi Shrine] (official website) {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine haiden.jpeg|270px]]
Worship Hall | location = 116 Nishinoyama Sakuranobabachō, Yamashina-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|58|12|N|135|47|44|E|}} | Deities = [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] | consecration_year = [[:en:1935|1935]] ([[Shōwa]] 10) | type = Former [[:en:Fusha|Fusha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | Main Festival = [[April 14]] (Spring Grand Festival), [[December 14]] (Gishi Festival) | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=12|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=44|label=Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]]. It enshrines [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Yoshio]], also known as Ōishi Kuranosuke, the leader of the [[:en:Akō Rōshi|Akō Rōshi]] (47 Ronin). The shrine is situated near the location where Ōishi Yoshio lived in seclusion from July of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 14 (1701) to September of Genroku 15 (1702) while planning the [[revenge]] of their lord. The shrine attracts worshippers who seek blessings for "great wish fulfillment" (大願成就), inspired by the determination of its enshrined deity. == History == In the early [[Shōwa]] era, a rōkyoku storyteller, Yoshida Yamato-no-Jō (second-generation [[Yoshida Narimaru]]), who deeply revered the Akō Rōshi, initiated the construction of a shrine dedicated to Ōishi Yoshio. With the support of local and governmental organizations, including the Governor of Kyoto serving as chair of the shrine construction committee, fundraising efforts led to the Completion tier 2]] (Shōwa 10). The shrine received permission for its establishment on [[December 9, 1933]] (Shōwa 8) and was designated as a [[:en:Fusha|Fusha]] in [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12). == Features == * **Main Hall** - Built in [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Worship Hall** - Built in [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Gijinsya (Loyalty Shrine)** - A subordinate shrine enshrining [[Amano-ya Rihei]], who is said to have provided weapons for the assault on [[:en:Kira Yoshinaka|Kira Yoshinaka]]. * **Treasure Hall** - Houses artifacts and memorabilia associated with the Akō Rōshi. * **Administration Office** - Handles shrine operations. === Miniature Horse === The shrine keeps a miniature horse named "Hanako," which has been cared for within the precincts since [[:en:2007|2007]] (Heisei 19). == Festivals and Events == * **April 14** - Spring Grand Festival * **December 14** - Gishi Festival, commemorating the Akō Rōshi's raid. == Access == * 1-minute walk from the Ōishi Shrine-mae [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]] stop. * 20 minutes by car from the [[Kyoto Higashi Interchange]] on the [[Meishin Expressway]]. * Close to [[Shinjūjō Street]] and [[Inariyama Tunnel]]. == External Links == * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ Official Website of Ōishi Shrine] [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|Main Hall|left]] {{Clear}} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * **Black Thread Armor** and [[Human Mukotsu]] replica - owned by [[Mori Nagayoshi]] ([https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 Human Mukotsu replica forged by Akō swordsmith Sasaki Noriyuki during the late Edo period]). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大石神社|version=104543748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: of the shrine buildings in [[:en:1935|1935]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] }} [[sn:Oishi Shrine]] [[ja:大石神社]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Oishi Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:National historic sites in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:赤穂事件]] [[Category:Buildings in Akō]] [[Category:History of Ako, Hyogo]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruses}} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Yoshio]] and the [[Akō Rōnin]] (Akō Gishi), who carried out the raid in the [[:en:Akō incident|Akō incident]]. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], open veneration was impossible due to restrictions by the [[:en:Edo shogunate|Edo shogunate]]. However, in [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of the [[Meiji]] era), [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] sent an imperial envoy to [[Sengaku-ji]], where the Akō Rōnin are buried, to pay tribute. This inspired movements to establish a shrine within Akō Castle. In the late [[Taishō]] and early [[Shōwa]] periods, shrines dedicated to the Akō Rōnin were established in [[Akō City]] and [[:en:Kyōto City|Kyōto City]]. It is one of the shrines that became popular in the late Edo period for venerating [[:en:Feudal lords|Feudal lords]]. == Akō Ōishi Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Akō Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyōgo pref Japan02s3.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location=[[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Akō City]], Upper Kariya, Former Castle Grounds | coordinates={{Coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}} | deity=Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio and the 47 loyal retainers, [[Kayano Shigemitsu|Kayano Sanpei]], Three Generations of the Asano Clan, Seven Generals of the Mori Clan | Rank=Former [[Prefectural Shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | Established=[[:en:1900|1900]] (Meiji 33) | Pilgrimage Route=[[Sacred Sites of Kami and Buddhas Pilgrimage]] No. 76 (Hyōgo No. 11) | Festival=[[December 14]] ([[Akō Gishi Festival]]) | Map={{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=57|lon_deg=134|lon_min=23|lon_sec=19|label=Akō Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}}}} }} {{Commonscat|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} Located in [[:en:Akō Castle|Akō Castle]] in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Akō City]], the shrine is a merger of Akō Shrine and Ōishi Shrine. Its former [[modern shrine Rank system|Rank]] was prefectural, and it is now listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The principal deities enshrined are the ancestral spirits of the [[:en:Asano clan|Asano clan]] and [[Mori clan]], former [[:en:Akō Domain|Akō Domain]] lords, as well as [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Yoshio]] and the 47 loyal retainers, including [[Kayano Shigemitsu]], who committed seppuku midway through the raid. As the shrine is associated with fulfilling the great wish of avenging a lord, it is believed to grant blessings for "great wish fulfillment." === History === After the Akō incident, followers of the Akō Rōnin secretly enshrined them in a small shrine within the former Ōishi residence in Akō Castle. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the castle was sold, and the buildings were demolished, leaving the site desolate due to floods from the [[Chikusa River]]. In [[:en:1900|1900]] (Meiji 33), the government authorized the establishment of "Ōishi Shrine," but fundraising efforts for construction faced difficulties.Katayama Hakusen, "Senkai Oshō Nikki-shō" (Kagakuji, 1967) Opposition also came from [[right-wing]] and [[nationalist]] figures such as {{ill|Ōmachi Keigetsu|ja|大町桂月|ca|Ōmachi Keigetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmachi Keigetsu|qq=}}.Seikyosha, "Nihon oyobi Nihonjin" (1909) Construction finally began in April [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43), and the shrine was completed in [[:en:1912|1912]] (Taishō 1). In [[:en:1928|1928]] (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to prefectural shrine status. After [[World War II]], the shrine merged with other shrines within and outside the castle grounds, including those dedicated to the Asano and Mori clans, as well as Inari Shrine and Ebisu Shrine, and was newly established as '''Akō Ōishi Shrine''' in [[:en:2000|2000]] (Heisei 12). === Korean Dogs === * The grounds feature statues of [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (Korean dogs), which are closer in appearance to dogs than the lion-like [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] typically found at other shrines. * The shrine houses historical artifacts such as a "Dog Receipt Certificate,"Wakisaka family documents: "Record of Dogs in the Castle Grounds" illustrating the historical connection between the Asano family and dogs in the Akō Domain. This shrine is one of the few in Japan that allows visitors to bring dogs. === Grounds === The shrine complex includes the main shrine, the worship hall, and several subordinate shrines. Facilities such as the Gishi Treasure Hall display artifacts related to the Akō Rōnin, while the Gishi Statue Hall exhibits wooden statues of the 47 retainers. * Main Hall – Built in [[:en:1912|1912]] (Taishō 1). * Worship Hall – Built in 1912. * Shrine Office * Subordinate Shrine Hall – Includes shrines such as [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrine]], [[Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|Awashima Shrine]], and [[Tenmangū]]. * Gishi Historical Museum – A member of the [[Japan Museums Association]].[https://www.j-muse.or.jp/introduction/detail/?mid=2942 Akō Ōishi Shrine Gishi Historical Museum] Japan Museums Association Features a collection of relics related to the Akō Rōnin. * Gishi Statue Hall – Displays 49 sculptures of the loyal retainers by contemporary sculptors. === Cultural Properties === ==== National Historic Site ==== * Former Residence of Ōishi Yoshio – Managed by Akō City. === Festivals and Annual Events === * January 15 – [[Tondo]] Festival * April, 2nd Sunday – Spring Gishi Festival * May 5 – Ōishi Mochi Festival * June 30 – Rice Planting Festival * October, Sunday – Rice Harvesting Festival * December 14 – [[Akō Gishi Festival]] === Access === * 15-minute walk from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]'s [[:en:Akō Line|Akō Line]] [[:en:Banshū-Akō Station|Banshū-Akō Station]]. * 10-minute drive from [[Sanyō Expressway]] [[Akō Interchange]].Official shrine website, "History" section. === External Links === * [http://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/ Akō Ōishi Shrine] (official website) {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine haiden.jpeg|270px]]
Worship Hall | location = 116 Nishinoyama Sakuranobabachō, Yamashina-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|58|12|N|135|47|44|E|}} | Deities = [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] | consecration_year = [[:en:1935|1935]] ([[Shōwa]] 10) | type = Former [[:en:Fusha|Fusha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | Main Festival = [[April 14]] (Spring Grand Festival), [[December 14]] (Gishi Festival) | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=12|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=44|label=Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]]. It enshrines [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Yoshio]], also known as Ōishi Kuranosuke, the leader of the [[:en:Akō Rōshi|Akō Rōshi]] (47 Ronin). The shrine is situated near the location where Ōishi Yoshio lived in seclusion from July of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 14 (1701) to September of Genroku 15 (1702) while planning the [[revenge]] of their lord. The shrine attracts worshippers who seek blessings for "great wish fulfillment" (大願成就), inspired by the determination of its enshrined deity. == History == In the early [[Shōwa]] era, a rōkyoku storyteller, Yoshida Yamato-no-Jō (second-generation [[Yoshida Narimaru]]), who deeply revered the Akō Rōshi, initiated the construction of a shrine dedicated to Ōishi Yoshio. With the support of local and governmental organizations, including the Governor of Kyoto serving as chair of the shrine construction committee, fundraising efforts led to the Completion tier 2]] (Shōwa 10). The shrine received permission for its establishment on [[December 9, 1933]] (Shōwa 8) and was designated as a [[:en:Fusha|Fusha]] in [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12). == Features == * **Main Hall** - Built in [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Worship Hall** - Built in [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Gijinsya (Loyalty Shrine)** - A subordinate shrine enshrining [[Amano-ya Rihei]], who is said to have provided weapons for the assault on [[:en:Kira Yoshinaka|Kira Yoshinaka]]. * **Treasure Hall** - Houses artifacts and memorabilia associated with the Akō Rōshi. * **Administration Office** - Handles shrine operations. === Miniature Horse === The shrine keeps a miniature horse named "Hanako," which has been cared for within the precincts since [[:en:2007|2007]] (Heisei 19). == Festivals and Events == * **April 14** - Spring Grand Festival * **December 14** - Gishi Festival, commemorating the Akō Rōshi's raid. == Access == * 1-minute walk from the Ōishi Shrine-mae [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]] stop. * 20 minutes by car from the [[Kyoto Higashi Interchange]] on the [[Meishin Expressway]]. * Close to [[Shinjūjō Street]] and [[Inariyama Tunnel]]. == External Links == * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ Official Website of Ōishi Shrine] [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|Main Hall|left]] {{Clear}} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * **Black Thread Armor** and [[Human Mukotsu]] replica - owned by [[Mori Nagayoshi]] ([https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 Human Mukotsu replica forged by Akō swordsmith Sasaki Noriyuki during the late Edo period]). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大石神社|version=104543748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: of the shrine buildings in [[:en:1935|1935]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] }} [[sn:Oishi Shrine]] [[ja:大石神社]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Oishi Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:National historic sites in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:赤穂事件]] [[Category:Buildings in Akō]] [[Category:History of Ako, Hyogo]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]]

Houses artifacts and memorabilia associated with the Akō Rōshi. * **Administration Office** - Handles shrine operations. === Miniature Horse === The shrine keeps a miniature horse named "Hanako," which has been cared for within the precincts since [[:en:2007|2007]] (Heisei 19). == Festivals and Events == * **April 14** - Spring Grand Festival * **December 14** - Gishi Festival, commemorating the Akō Rōshi's raid. == Access == * 1-minute walk from the Ōishi Shrine-mae [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]] stop. * 20 minutes by car from the [[Kyoto Higashi Interchange]] on the [[:en:Meishin Expressway|Meishin Expressway]]. * Close to [[Shinjūjō Street]] and [[Inariyama Tunnel]]. == External Links == * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ Official Website of Ōishi Shrine] [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|Main Hall|left]] {{Clear}} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * **Black Thread Armor** and [[Human Mukotsu]] replica - owned by [[Mori Nagayoshi]] ([https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 Human Mukotsu replica forged by Akō swordsmith Sasaki Noriyuki during the late Edo period]). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大石神社|version=104543748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: of the shrine buildings in [[:en:1935|1935]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] }} [[sn:Oishi Shrine]] [[ja:大石神社]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Oishi Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:National historic sites in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:赤穂事件]] [[Category:Buildings in Akō]] [[Category:History of Ako, Hyogo]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{Otheruses}} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[shrine]] dedicated to [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Yoshio]] and the [[Akō Rōnin]] (Akō Gishi), who carried out the raid in the [[:en:Akō incident|Akō incident]]. During the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], open veneration was impossible due to restrictions by the [[:en:Edo shogunate|Edo shogunate]]. However, in [[:en:1868|1868]] (the first year of the [[Meiji]] era), [[:en:Emperor Meiji|Emperor Meiji]] sent an imperial envoy to [[Sengaku-ji]], where the Akō Rōnin are buried, to pay tribute. This inspired movements to establish a shrine within Akō Castle. In the late [[Taishō]] and early [[Shōwa]] periods, shrines dedicated to the Akō Rōnin were established in [[Akō City]] and [[:en:Kyōto City|Kyōto City]]. It is one of the shrines that became popular in the late Edo period for venerating [[:en:Feudal lords|Feudal lords]]. == Akō Ōishi Shrine == {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Akō Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:141206 Oishi-jinja Ako Hyōgo pref Japan02s3.jpg|250px]]
Haiden | location=[[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Akō City]], Upper Kariya, Former Castle Grounds | coordinates={{Coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}} | deity=Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio and the 47 loyal retainers, [[Kayano Shigemitsu|Kayano Sanpei]], Three Generations of the Asano Clan, Seven Generals of the Mori Clan | Rank=Former [[Prefectural Shrine]]; [[Beppyo Shrine]] | Established=[[:en:1900|1900]] (Meiji 33) | Pilgrimage Route=[[Sacred Sites of Kami and Buddhas Pilgrimage]] No. 76 (Hyōgo No. 11) | Festival=[[December 14]] ([[Akō Gishi Festival]]) | Map={{location map|Japan Hyōgo|lat_deg=34|lat_min=44|lat_sec=57|lon_deg=134|lon_min=23|lon_sec=19|label=Akō Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|44|57|N|134|23|19|E}}}} }} {{Commonscat|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} Located in [[:en:Akō Castle|Akō Castle]] in [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]], [[Akō City]], the shrine is a merger of Akō Shrine and Ōishi Shrine. Its former [[modern shrine Rank system|Rank]] was prefectural, and it is now listed as a [[Beppyo Shrine]] under the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. The principal deities enshrined are the ancestral spirits of the [[:en:Asano clan|Asano clan]] and [[Mori clan]], former [[:en:Akō Domain|Akō Domain]] lords, as well as [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Yoshio]] and the 47 loyal retainers, including [[Kayano Shigemitsu]], who committed seppuku midway through the raid. As the shrine is associated with fulfilling the great wish of avenging a lord, it is believed to grant blessings for "great wish fulfillment." === History === After the Akō incident, followers of the Akō Rōnin secretly enshrined them in a small shrine within the former Ōishi residence in Akō Castle. Following the [[Meiji Restoration]], the castle was sold, and the buildings were demolished, leaving the site desolate due to floods from the [[Chikusa River]]. In [[:en:1900|1900]] (Meiji 33), the government authorized the establishment of "Ōishi Shrine," but fundraising efforts for construction faced difficulties.Katayama Hakusen, "Senkai Oshō Nikki-shō" (Kagakuji, 1967) Opposition also came from [[right-wing]] and [[:en:Nationalist|Nationalist]] figures such as {{ill|Ōmachi Keigetsu|ja|大町桂月|ca|Ōmachi Keigetsu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmachi Keigetsu|qq=}}.Seikyosha, "Nihon oyobi Nihonjin" (1909) Construction finally began in April [[:en:1910|1910]] (Meiji 43), and the shrine was completed in [[:en:1912|1912]] (Taishō 1). In [[:en:1928|1928]] (Shōwa 3), it was promoted to prefectural shrine status. After [[World War II]], the shrine merged with other shrines within and outside the castle grounds, including those dedicated to the Asano and Mori clans, as well as Inari Shrine and Ebisu Shrine, and was newly established as '''Akō Ōishi Shrine''' in [[:en:2000|2000]] (Heisei 12). === Korean Dogs === * The grounds feature statues of [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] (Korean dogs), which are closer in appearance to dogs than the lion-like [[:en:Komainu|Komainu]] typically found at other shrines. * The shrine houses historical artifacts such as a "Dog Receipt Certificate,"Wakisaka family documents: "Record of Dogs in the Castle Grounds" illustrating the historical connection between the Asano family and dogs in the Akō Domain. This shrine is one of the few in Japan that allows visitors to bring dogs. === Grounds === The shrine complex includes the main shrine, the worship hall, and several subordinate shrines. Facilities such as the Gishi Treasure Hall display artifacts related to the Akō Rōnin, while the Gishi Statue Hall exhibits wooden statues of the 47 retainers. * Main Hall – Built in [[:en:1912|1912]] (Taishō 1). * Worship Hall – Built in 1912. * Shrine Office * Subordinate Shrine Hall – Includes shrines such as [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari shrine]], [[Awashima Shrine (Wakayama)|Awashima Shrine]], and [[Tenmangū]]. * Gishi Historical Museum – A member of the [[Japan Museums Association]].[https://www.j-muse.or.jp/introduction/detail/?mid=2942 Akō Ōishi Shrine Gishi Historical Museum] Japan Museums Association Features a collection of relics related to the Akō Rōnin. * Gishi Statue Hall – Displays 49 sculptures of the loyal retainers by contemporary sculptors. === Cultural Properties === ==== National Historic Site ==== * Former Residence of Ōishi Yoshio – Managed by Akō City. === Festivals and Annual Events === * January 15 – [[Tondo]] Festival * April, 2nd Sunday – Spring Gishi Festival * May 5 – Ōishi Mochi Festival * June 30 – Rice Planting Festival * October, Sunday – Rice Harvesting Festival * December 14 – [[Akō Gishi Festival]] === Access === * 15-minute walk from [[:en:West Japan Railway Company|West Japan Railway Company]]'s [[:en:Akō Line|Akō Line]] [[:en:Banshū-Akō Station|Banshū-Akō Station]]. * 10-minute drive from [[Sanyō Expressway]] [[Akō Interchange]].Official shrine website, "History" section. === External Links === * [http://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/ Akō Ōishi Shrine] (official website) {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Ōishi Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine haiden.jpeg|270px]]
Worship Hall | location = 116 Nishinoyama Sakuranobabachō, Yamashina-ku, Kyoto City, Kyoto Prefecture | coordinates = {{Coord|34|58|12|N|135|47|44|E|}} | Deities = [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] | consecration_year = [[:en:1935|1935]] ([[Shōwa]] 10) | type = Former [[:en:Fusha|Fusha]] (Prefectural Shrine) | Main Festival = [[April 14]] (Spring Grand Festival), [[December 14]] (Gishi Festival) | Main Hall = Nagare-zukuri | Map = {{location map|Japan Kyoto city|lat_deg=34|lat_min=58|lat_sec=12|lon_deg=135|lon_min=47|lon_sec=44|label=Ōishi Shrine|position=center|width=200|float=center}} }} '''Ōishi Shrine''' (大石神社) is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto City|Kyoto City]]. It enshrines [[:en:Ōishi Yoshio|Ōishi Yoshio]], also known as Ōishi Kuranosuke, the leader of the [[:en:Akō Rōshi|Akō Rōshi]] (47 Ronin). The shrine is situated near the location where Ōishi Yoshio lived in seclusion from July of [[:en:Genroku|Genroku]] 14 (1701) to September of Genroku 15 (1702) while planning the [[revenge]] of their lord. The shrine attracts worshippers who seek blessings for "great wish fulfillment" (大願成就), inspired by the determination of its enshrined deity. == History == In the early [[Shōwa]] era, a rōkyoku storyteller, Yoshida Yamato-no-Jō (second-generation [[Yoshida Narimaru]]), who deeply revered the Akō Rōshi, initiated the construction of a shrine dedicated to Ōishi Yoshio. With the support of local and governmental organizations, including the Governor of Kyoto serving as chair of the shrine construction committee, fundraising efforts led to the Completion tier 2]] (Shōwa 10). The shrine received permission for its establishment on [[December 9, 1933]] (Shōwa 8) and was designated as a [[:en:Fusha|Fusha]] in [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12). == Features == * **Main Hall** - Built in [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Worship Hall** - Built in [[:en:1935|1935]] (Shōwa 10). * **Gijinsya (Loyalty Shrine)** - A subordinate shrine enshrining [[Amano-ya Rihei]], who is said to have provided weapons for the assault on [[:en:Kira Yoshinaka|Kira Yoshinaka]]. * **Treasure Hall** - Houses artifacts and memorabilia associated with the Akō Rōshi. * **Administration Office** - Handles shrine operations. === Miniature Horse === The shrine keeps a miniature horse named "Hanako," which has been cared for within the precincts since [[:en:2007|2007]] (Heisei 19). == Festivals and Events == * **April 14** - Spring Grand Festival * **December 14** - Gishi Festival, commemorating the Akō Rōshi's raid. == Access == * 1-minute walk from the Ōishi Shrine-mae [[:en:Keihan Bus|Keihan Bus]] stop. * 20 minutes by car from the [[Kyoto Higashi Interchange]] on the [[:en:Meishin Expressway|Meishin Expressway]]. * Close to [[Shinjūjō Street]] and [[Inariyama Tunnel]]. == External Links == * [http://www.ooishijinja.com/ Official Website of Ōishi Shrine] [[File:Kyoto Oishi Shrine honden.jpeg|thumb|Main Hall|left]] {{Clear}} == References == {{Footnotes}} {{Reflist}} == See Also == * **Black Thread Armor** and [[Human Mukotsu]] replica - owned by [[Mori Nagayoshi]] ([https://www.ako-ooishijinjya.or.jp/news/939 Human Mukotsu replica forged by Akō swordsmith Sasaki Noriyuki during the late Edo period]). {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|大石神社|version=104543748|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: of the shrine buildings in [[:en:1935|1935]] [[Category:Former Fu-sha in Kyoto]] [[Category:Fu-sha]] [[Category:1935 establishments in Japan]] }} [[sn:Oishi Shrine]] [[ja:大石神社]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E7%9F%B3%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Oishi Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kyoto]] [[Category:National historic sites in Hyogo prefecture]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hyōgo Prefecture]] [[Category:Ōishi Kuranosuke Yoshio]] [[Category:Buildings in Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:History of Yamashina-ku, Kyoto]] [[Category:赤穂事件]] [[Category:Buildings in Akō]] [[Category:History of Ako, Hyogo]] [[Category:translated pages with valid en interwikis]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]] {{Commons category|Ako-Oishi-jinja}} [[fa:معبد اوایشی]]
